pages.italian_text: Un giorno, un uomo stava camminando lungo una strada. Aveva solo una vecchia borsa nelle mani. Si chiedeva perché le persone che possedevano tanto denaro non fossero mai soddisfatte e desiderassero sempre di più. “Per quanto mi riguarda”, disse, “se solo avessi abbastanza da mangiare, non chiederei niente altro”. In quello stesso istante, Fortuna passeggiava lungo la stessa strada. Sentì le parole dell’uomo e si fermò. “Senti,” disse lei, “Voglio aiutarti. Apri la borsa e ci verserò dei diamanti. Ma ogni diamante che cadrà a terra si trasformerà in polvere. Hai capito?” “Oh, si, ho capito,” rispose l’uomo.
pages.english_text: One day a man was walking along the street. He had only an old bag in his hands. He was wondering why people who had a lot of money were never satisfied and always wanted more. “As far as I’m concerned”, he said, “if I only had enough to eat, I would not ask for anything else”. Just at this moment Fortune came down the street. She heard the man and stopped. “Listen,” she said, “I want to help you. Open your bag and I will pour diamonds into it. But every diamond which falls on the ground will become dust. Do you understand?” “Oh, yes, I understand,” replied the man.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Si chiedeva perché le persone che possedevano tanto denaro non fossero mai soddisfatte'.
translation.answer: He was wondering why people who had a lot of money were never satisfied.

comprehension.question: What did the man say he would ask for if he had enough to eat?
comprehension.answer: He would not ask for anything else.

pages.italian_text: Egli aprì velocemente la sua borsa e vide una corrente di diamanti riversarsi in essa. La borsa iniziò a diventare pesante. “È sufficiente?” chiese Fortuna. “Non ancora.” Le mani dell’uomo iniziarono a tremare. “Ora, sei l’uomo più ricco del mondo,” disse Fortuna. “Ancora un po’, aggiungine ancora un po’,” disse l’uomo. Venne aggiunto un altro diamante e la vecchia borsa si strappò. Tutti i diamanti caddero a terra e si trasformarono in polvere. Fortuna sparì, lasciando l’uomo solo per la strada.
pages.english_text: He quickly opened his bag and saw a stream of diamonds was poured into it. The bag began to grow heavy. “Is that enough?” asked Fortune. “Not yet.” The man’s hands began to tremble. “You are the richest man in the world now,” said Fortune. “Just a few more, add a few more,” said the man. Another diamond was added and the old bag split. All the diamonds fell on the ground and became dust. Fortune disappeared, leaving the man along on the street.

translation.question: Translate into Italian: 'The bag began to grow heavy.'
translation.answer: La borsa iniziò a diventare pesante.

comprehension.question: What happened to the diamonds that fell on the ground?
comprehension.answer: They became dust.

comprehension.question: Why did the bag split?
comprehension.answer: Because the man kept asking for more diamonds and another diamond was added.

overall_questions.question: What was the man carrying when he met Fortune?
overall_questions.answer: An old bag.

overall_questions.question: What did the man say he would ask for if he had enough to eat?
overall_questions.answer: He would not ask for anything else.

overall_questions.question: What condition did Fortune give about the diamonds?
overall_questions.answer: Every diamond that falls on the ground will become dust.

overall_questions.question: Did the man stop asking for more diamonds when Fortune said he was the richest man?
overall_questions.answer: No, he asked for a few more.

overall_questions.question: What happened to the bag when another diamond was added?
overall_questions.answer: The old bag split.

overall_questions.question: What happened to all the diamonds after the bag split?
overall_questions.answer: They fell on the ground and became dust.

overall_questions.question: What did Fortune do after the diamonds turned to dust?
overall_questions.answer: She disappeared.

overall_questions.question: Was the man left with any diamonds at the end?
overall_questions.answer: No, he had nothing.

overall_questions.question: What lesson does this story teach?
overall_questions.answer: Being greedy can cause you to lose everything.

overall_questions.question: Who was walking along the street when the man was talking to himself?
overall_questions.answer: Fortune.

pages.italian_text: Un giorno, un vecchio gentiluomo andò dal medico. Il medico lo visitò e disse: “La medicina non l’aiuterà. Lei ha bisogno di riposo assoluto. Vada in un paese tranquillo per un mese, cammini molto, beva latte, vada a dormire presto e fumi soltanto un sigaro al giorno.” “Grazie mille,” disse il vecchio gentiluomo, “Farò tutto quello che mi ha detto.”
pages.english_text: One time an old gentleman went to see a doctor. The doctor examined him and said: “Medicine won’t help you. You must have a complete rest. Go to a quiet country place for a month, walk a lot, drink milk, go to bed early, and smoke just one cigar a day.” “Thank you very much,” said the old gentleman, “I shall do everything you say.”

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Vada in un paese tranquillo per un mese.'
translation.answer: Go to a quiet country place for a month.

comprehension.question: What did the doctor tell the old gentleman to drink?
comprehension.answer: Milk.

comprehension.question: How many cigars per day did the doctor allow?
comprehension.answer: One cigar a day.

pages.italian_text: Un mese più tardi, il gentiluomo ritornò dal medico. “Come si sente ora?” chiese il medico, “Mi fa piacere rivederla. Sembra molto più giovane.” “Oh, dottore,” disse il gentiluomo, “Mi sento abbastanza bene ora. Mi sono riposato. Sono andato a letto presto, ho bevuto molto latte e ho camminato molto. Il suo consiglio mi ha sicuramente aiutato. Ma mi ha detto di fumare un sigaro al giorno, e quell’unico sigaro al giorno mi ha quasi ucciso all’inizio. Non è uno scherzo iniziare a fumare alla mia età.
pages.english_text: A month later the gentleman came to the doctor again. “How do you feel?” said the doctor, “I am very glad to see you. You look much younger.” “Oh, doctor,” said the gentleman, “I feel quite well now. I had a good rest. I went to bed early, I drank a lot of milk, and I walked a lot. Your advice certainly helped me. But you told me to smoke one cigar a day, and that one cigar a day almost killed me at first. It’s no joke to start smoking at my age.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Sembra molto più giovane.'
translation.answer: You look much younger.

comprehension.question: Did the old gentleman follow all the doctor's advice?
comprehension.answer: Yes, he rested, went to bed early, drank milk, walked a lot, and smoked one cigar a day.

comprehension.question: What problem did he have with the cigar?
comprehension.answer: It almost killed him at first because it’s no joke to start smoking at his age.

overall_questions.question: Why did the old gentleman go to the doctor?
overall_questions.answer: He was ill (he needed a complete rest).

overall_questions.question: What did the doctor say about medicine?
overall_questions.answer: Medicine wouldn't help him.

overall_questions.question: What four things did the doctor tell him to do?
overall_questions.answer: Go to a quiet country place, walk a lot, drink milk, go to bed early.

overall_questions.question: How many cigars per day did the doctor allow?
overall_questions.answer: One cigar a day.

overall_questions.question: How did the old gentleman feel a month later?
overall_questions.answer: Quite well.

overall_questions.question: What did the doctor observe about his appearance?
overall_questions.answer: He looked much younger.

overall_questions.question: What did the gentleman say about the one cigar a day?
overall_questions.answer: It almost killed him at first.

overall_questions.question: Why was smoking hard for him?
overall_questions.answer: Because starting to smoke at his age is no joke.

overall_questions.question: Did the gentleman follow the doctor's advice completely?
overall_questions.answer: Yes, he did everything the doctor said.

overall_questions.question: What is the main humor in this story?
overall_questions.answer: The doctor's advice to smoke one cigar a day was the most difficult part for the old man, even though it seemed simple.

pages.italian_text: Un turista era in piedi davanti alla reception di un hotel a Washington. Aveva fretta. Aveva solo dieci minuti per pagare il conto e arrivare alla stazione. Improvvisamente si ricordò di aver dimenticato qualcosa. Chiamò il fattorino e disse: 'Corri su nella stanza 85 e vedi se ho lasciato una scatola sopra al tavolo. Fai veloce per favore, ho fretta.'
pages.english_text: A tourist was standing in front of the reception desk of a Washington hotel. He was in a hurry. He had only ten minutes to pay his bill and arrive at the station. Suddenly he remembered that he had forgotten something. He called the bellboy and said: 'Run up to room 85 and see whether I left a box on the table. Be quick please, I am in a hurry.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Aveva solo dieci minuti per pagare il conto e arrivare alla stazione.'
translation.answer: He had only ten minutes to pay his bill and arrive at the station.

comprehension.question: Where was the tourist?
comprehension.answer: In front of the reception desk of a Washington hotel.

comprehension.question: Why was he in a hurry?
comprehension.answer: He had only ten minutes to pay his bill and arrive at the station.

pages.italian_text: Il ragazzo corse su per le scale. Passarono cinque minuti, mentre il gentiluomo camminava su e giù impazientemente. Finalmente il ragazzo ritornò. 'Si, signore,' disse, 'I'ha lasciata lì. È sopra al tavolo.'
pages.english_text: The boy ran up the stairs. Five minutes passed, while gentleman was walking up and down impatiently. At last the boy came back. 'Yes, sir,' he said, 'you left it there. It's on the table.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Il ragazzo corse su per le scale.'
translation.answer: The boy ran up the stairs.

comprehension.question: How long did the boy take?
comprehension.answer: Five minutes.

comprehension.question: What did the boy confirm?
comprehension.answer: That the box was on the table.

overall_questions.question: Why did the tourist call the bellboy?
overall_questions.answer: To run up to room 85 and see if he left a box on the table.

overall_questions.question: How much time did the tourist have to pay his bill and reach the station?
overall_questions.answer: Ten minutes.

overall_questions.question: What did the tourist do while waiting for the bellboy?
overall_questions.answer: He walked up and down impatiently.

overall_questions.question: How long did the boy take to come back?
overall_questions.answer: Five minutes.

overall_questions.question: What did the boy report?
overall_questions.answer: That the tourist had left the box on the table.

overall_questions.question: Did the boy bring the box back?
overall_questions.answer: No, he only confirmed it was there.

overall_questions.question: What is funny or ironic about this story?
overall_questions.answer: The tourist sent the boy to check if he left a box, but didn't ask him to bring it, wasting time.

overall_questions.question: Where was the tourist standing?
overall_questions.answer: In front of the reception desk of a Washington hotel.

overall_questions.question: What was the tourist's urgency?
overall_questions.answer: He had only ten minutes to pay his bill and get to the station.

overall_questions.question: What did the tourist forget?
overall_questions.answer: A box on the table in room 85.

pages.italian_text: Aesop era un uomo molto intelligente vissuto in Grecia molte centinaia di anni fa. Scrisse molte belle storie. Era molto conosciuto come uomo a cui piacevano gli scherzi. Un giorno, mentre si godeva una passeggiata, incontrò un viaggiatore, che lo salutò e disse: 'Signore, può dirmi quanto mi ci vorrà per arrivare in città?' 'Vada,' Aesop rispose. 'Lo so che devo andare,' protestò il viaggiatore, 'ma voglio che lei mi dica quanto tempo mi ci vuole per arrivare in città?'
pages.english_text: Aesop was a very clever man who lived many hundreds of years ago in Greece. He wrote many fine stories. He was well-known as a man who liked jokes. One day, as he was enjoying a walk, he met a traveler, who greeted him and said: 'Signor, can you tell me how soon I shall get to town?' 'Go,' Aesop answered. 'I know I must go,' protested the traveler, 'but I want you to tell me how soon I shall get to town?'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Aesop era un uomo molto intelligente.'
translation.answer: Aesop was a very clever man.

comprehension.question: What was Aesop known for?
comprehension.answer: He liked jokes.

comprehension.question: What did the traveler ask Aesop?
comprehension.answer: How soon he would get to town.

pages.italian_text: 'Vada,' rispose di nuovo Aesop arrabbiato. 'Quest’uomo deve essere pazzo,' pensò il viaggiatore e se ne andò. Dopo che ebbe camminato una certa distanza, Aesop gli urlò: 'Ci vorranno due ore per arrivare in città.' Il viaggiatore si girò sbalordito. 'E perché non me lo ha detto prima?' chiese. 'Come avrei potuto dirglielo prima?' rispose Aesop. 'Non sapevo quanto veloce lei camminasse.'
pages.english_text: 'Go,' Aesop said again angrily. 'This man must be mad,' the traveler thought and went on. After he had walked some distance, Aesop shouted after him: 'It will take you two hours to get to town.' The traveler turned around in astonishment. 'Why didn’t you tell me that before?' he asked. 'How could I have told you that before?' answered Aesop. 'I did not know how fast you can walk.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Ci vorranno due ore per arrivare in città.'
translation.answer: It will take you two hours to get to town.

comprehension.question: What did Aesop finally tell the traveler after he had walked some distance?
comprehension.answer: That it would take him two hours to get to town.

comprehension.question: Why didn't Aesop answer earlier?
comprehension.answer: Because he didn't know how fast the traveler could walk.

overall_questions.question: Who was Aesop?
overall_questions.answer: A very clever man who lived in Greece many hundreds of years ago, known for his stories and jokes.

overall_questions.question: What did the traveler ask Aesop?
overall_questions.answer: How soon he would get to town.

overall_questions.question: How did Aesop first answer?
overall_questions.answer: He simply said 'Go'.

overall_questions.question: What did the traveler think of Aesop?
overall_questions.answer: He thought Aesop must be mad.

overall_questions.question: When did Aesop give the actual time?
overall_questions.answer: After the traveler had walked some distance.

overall_questions.question: How long would it take the traveler to get to town?
overall_questions.answer: Two hours.

overall_questions.question: Why couldn't Aesop tell him before?
overall_questions.answer: Because he didn't know how fast the traveler could walk.

overall_questions.question: What is the moral of this story?
overall_questions.answer: You cannot give an accurate estimate of time without knowing the person's speed.

overall_questions.question: Did Aesop like jokes?
overall_questions.answer: Yes, he was well-known as a man who liked jokes.

overall_questions.question: How did the traveler react when Aesop shouted the time?
overall_questions.answer: He turned around in astonishment and asked why he hadn't said it before.

pages.italian_text: Un giorno, un uomo povero stava viaggiando a cavallo. Nel pomeriggio, quando si sentì stanco e affamato, si fermò, legò il suo cavallo ad un albero e si sedette per pranzare. Un uomo ricco arrivò nello stesso posto e iniziò a legare il suo cavallo allo stesso albero. 'Non legate il vostro cavallo a quel albero,' disse l'uomo povero, 'il mio cavallo è selvaggio e ucciderà il suo cavallo. Lo leghi ad un altro albero!' Ma l'uomo ricco rispose: 'Legherò il mio cavallo dove voglio!' Così, legò il suo cavallo e si sedette anche lui per mangiare.
pages.english_text: One day a poor man was travelling on horseback. In the afternoon, when he was tired and hungry, he stopped tied his horse to a tree and sat down to have his lunch. A rich man came to that place and began to tie his horse to the same tree. 'Do not tie your horse to that tree,' said the poor man, 'my horse is wild, it will kill your horse. Tie it to another tree!' But the rich man answered: 'I will tie my horse where I like!' So he tied up his horse and also sat down to eat his lunch.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Nel pomeriggio, quando si sentì stanco e affamato, si fermò.'
translation.answer: In the afternoon, when he was tired and hungry, he stopped.

comprehension.question: Why did the poor man warn the rich man?
comprehension.answer: Because his horse was wild and would kill the rich man's horse.

comprehension.question: Did the rich man listen to the warning?
comprehension.answer: No, he tied his horse where he liked.

pages.italian_text: Dopo alcuni minuti, sentirono un terribile rumore e videro che i due cavalli si stavano scontrando. Corsero verso di loro, ma era troppo tardi - il cavallo dell'uomo ricco era già stato ucciso. 'Hai visto cosa ha fatto il tuo cavallo!' urlò l'uomo ricco. 'Ora dovrà pagarmelo!' e portò l'uomo povero in tribunale. Il giudice si rivolse verso l'uomo povero e chiese: 'È vero che il suo cavallo ha ucciso il cavallo di lui?' Ma l'uomo povero non rispose.
pages.english_text: A few minutes later they heard a terrible noise and saw that the two horses were fighting. They ran up to them, but it was too late - the rich man's horse was killed. 'See what your horse has done!' cried the rich man. 'You will have to pay for it!' and he brought the poor man to the court. The judge turned to the poor man and asked: 'Is it true that your horse killed his horse?' But the poor man answered nothing.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Corsero verso di loro, ma era troppo tardi.'
translation.answer: They ran up to them, but it was too late.

comprehension.question: What happened to the rich man's horse?
comprehension.answer: It was killed.

comprehension.question: How did the poor man respond to the judge's first question?
comprehension.answer: He said nothing.

pages.italian_text: Poi il giudice ha chiesto all'uomo povero molte altre domande, ma lui non disse niente. Infine, il giudice urlò: 'Questo uomo è muto. Non può parlare!' 'Oh,' disse l'uomo ricco, 'lui sa parlare bene quanto lei e me. Mi ha parlato quando l'ho incontrato.' 'Ma ne è sicuro?' chiese il giudice. 'Certo che ne sono sicuro,' rispose l'uomo ricco. 'Mi ha detto di non legare il mio cavallo allo stesso albero dov'era legato il suo cavallo. Ha detto che il suo cavallo era selvaggio e avrebbe ucciso il mio cavallo.' 'Oh,' disse il giudice, 'ora vedo che lei ha torto. Lui l'aveva avvertito prima. Quindi non pagherà per il tuo cavallo.'
pages.english_text: Then the judge asked the poor man many other questions, but he did not say anything. At last the judge shouted: 'This man is dumb. He cannot speak!' 'Oh,' said the rich man, 'he can speak as well as you and I. He spoke to me when I met him.' 'Are you sure?' asked the judge. 'Of course I am sure,' answered the rich man. 'He told me not to tie my horse to the same tree where his horse was tied. He said his horse was wild and would kill my horse.' 'Oh,' said the judge, 'now I see that you are wrong. He warned you before. So he will not pay for your horse.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Lui l'aveva avvertito prima.'
translation.answer: He warned you before.

comprehension.question: What did the rich man reveal to the judge?
comprehension.answer: That the poor man had warned him not to tie his horse to the same tree.

comprehension.question: What was the judge's final decision?
comprehension.answer: The poor man would not pay for the rich man's horse.

pages.italian_text: Poi si rivolse verso l'uomo povero e gli chiese perché non avesse risposto a tutte le domande. L'uomo povero rispose: 'Non le ho risposto perché sapevo che avrebbe creduto di più ad un uomo ricco invece che ad un uomo povero. Così, volevo che fosse lui a raccontarvi tutto, e ora può vedere chi ha ragione e chi ha torto.'
pages.english_text: Then he turned to the poor man and asked him why he had not answered all questions. The poor man said: 'I did not answer you because I knew that you would believe a rich man rather than a poor man. So I wanted him to tell you everything, and now you see who was right and who was wrong.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Sapevo che avrebbe creduto di più ad un uomo ricco invece che ad un uomo povero.'
translation.answer: I knew that you would believe a rich man rather than a poor man.

comprehension.question: Why did the poor man stay silent?
comprehension.answer: Because he knew the judge would believe a rich man rather than a poor man, so he wanted the rich man to tell everything.

comprehension.question: Who ended up being right?
comprehension.answer: The poor man.

overall_questions.question: What was the poor man doing when the rich man arrived?
overall_questions.answer: He was having his lunch, with his horse tied to a tree.

overall_questions.question: What warning did the poor man give the rich man?
overall_questions.answer: That his horse was wild and would kill the rich man's horse, so he should tie his horse to another tree.

overall_questions.question: Did the rich man heed the warning?
overall_questions.answer: No, he tied his horse to the same tree.

overall_questions.question: What happened a few minutes later?
overall_questions.answer: The horses fought and the rich man's horse was killed.

overall_questions.question: What did the rich man do?
overall_questions.answer: He brought the poor man to court.

overall_questions.question: How did the poor man behave in court at first?
overall_questions.answer: He remained silent and did not answer any questions.

overall_questions.question: What did the rich man say when the judge thought the poor man was dumb?
overall_questions.answer: He said the poor man could speak and had warned him earlier.

overall_questions.question: What was the judge's verdict?
overall_questions.answer: The poor man would not have to pay because he had warned the rich man beforehand.

overall_questions.question: Why did the poor man finally explain his silence?
overall_questions.answer: He knew the judge would believe a rich man rather than a poor man, so he let the rich man tell the story.

overall_questions.question: What is the moral of this story?
overall_questions.answer: Sometimes it's wiser to let others incriminate themselves, and justice may prevail when the truth comes out.

pages.italian_text: Judson Webb era un uomo d'affari americano. Aveva un comodo appartamento a New York ma in estate lasciava la polverosa città per andare in campagna. Lì aveva un cottage composto da tre stanze, un bagno e una cucina. In una delle camere c'era un grande armadio. Al Signor Webb piaceva molto il suo cottage, soprattutto il suo armadio dove teneva i suoi fucili, le sue canne da pesca, il vino e altre cose. Judson Webb adorava i suoi averi e nemmeno sua moglie aveva il permesso di avere una chiave per quel armadio.
pages.english_text: Judson Webb was an American business man. He had a comfortable apartment in New York but in the summer he used to leave the dusty city and go to the country. There he had a cottage which consisted of three rooms, a bathroom and a kitchen. In one of the rooms there was a big closet. Mr. Webb liked his cottage very much, especially his closet where he kept his guns, fishing rods, wine and other things. Judson Webb loved his possessions and even his wife was not allowed to have a key to the closet.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Nemmeno sua moglie aveva il permesso di avere una chiave per quel armadio.'
translation.answer: Even his wife was not allowed to have a key to the closet.

comprehension.question: What did Judson Webb keep in his closet?
comprehension.answer: His guns, fishing rods, wine and other things.

comprehension.question: Who was not allowed to have a key to the closet?
comprehension.answer: His wife.

pages.italian_text: Era autunno, e Judson stava mettendo in valigia le sue cose per l'inverno. Tra pochi minuti sarebbe ritornato a New York. Mentre guardava la mensola dove di solito teneva il whiskey, la sua faccia si fece seria. Tutte le bottiglie erano chiuse eccetto una. Era stata disposta in modo invitante davanti con un bicchiere da whiskey a fianco. La bottiglia era meno di mezza piena. Mentre prendeva la bottiglia dalla mensola, Helen, sua moglie, gli parlò dalla stanza vicino: 'Ho messo tutto in valigia. Alec non è venuto a prendere le chiavi?' Alec viveva nelle vicinanze e faceva da custode.
pages.english_text: It was autumn now and Judson was packing his things for the winter. In a few minutes he would be going back to New York. As he looked at the shelf where he usually kept the whiskey, his face become serious. All the bottles were unopened except one. It was placed invitingly in front with a whiskey-glass by its side. The bottle was less than half full. As he took the bottle from the shelf, Helen, his wife, spoke from the next room: 'I've packed everything. Hasn't Alec come to get the keys?' Alec lived in the neighborhood and acted as caretaker.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Tutte le bottiglie erano chiuse eccetto una.'
translation.answer: All the bottles were unopened except one.

comprehension.question: What did Judson notice about the whiskey bottles?
comprehension.answer: All were unopened except one, which was less than half full.

comprehension.question: Who was Alec?
comprehension.answer: A caretaker living in the neighborhood.

pages.italian_text: 'È andato al lago a tirare fuori la barca dall'acqua. Ha detto che sarebbe ritornato in mezz'ora!' Helen entrò nella stanza con la valigia. Si fermò e rimase stupita vedendo la bottiglia nelle mani del marito. 'Judson,' esclamò, 'non stai bevendo alle dieci del mattino, vero?' 'No, cara. Non mi sto bevendo niente da questa bottiglia. Ci sto solo mettendo dentro qualcosa.' Prese due piccole pillole bianche dalla tasca e le mise sul tavolo. Poi aprì la bottiglia. 'La persona che ha forzato il mio armadio lo scorso inverno e ha rubato il mio whiskey, probabilmente ci riproverà mentre siamo via,' continuò, 'solo che questa volta se ne pentirà se prova a venire.'
pages.english_text: 'He's at the lake pulling the boat out of the water. He said he would be back in half an hour!' Helen came into the room carrying the suitcase. She stopped and looked in surprise as she saw the bottle in her husband's hand. 'Judson,' she exclaimed, 'you're not taking a drink at ten in the morning, are you?' 'No, dear. I'm not drinking anything out of this bottle. I am just putting something into it.' He took two small white pills out of his pocket and put them on the table. Then he opened the bottle. 'The person who broke into my closet last winter and stole my whiskey will probably try to do it again while we are away,' he went on, 'only this time he'll be very sorry if he comes.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Ci sto solo mettendo dentro qualcosa.'
translation.answer: I am just putting something into it.

comprehension.question: What did Judson take out of his pocket?
comprehension.answer: Two small white pills.

comprehension.question: Why was Judson putting pills into the whiskey bottle?
comprehension.answer: To punish the person who had stolen his whiskey before and might try again.

pages.italian_text: Poi, una alla volta, lasciò cadere le pillole nella bottiglia e la alzò per vederle sciogliere. Sua moglie lo guardò con orrore. 'Cosa sono?' finalmente gli chiese. 'Faranno male all'uomo?' 'Non solo male. Lo uccideranno,' rispose lui con soddisfazione. Chiuse la bottiglia e la rimise sulla mensola vicino al bicchiere da whiskey. Si sentiva soddisfatto. Poi disse: 'Ora, Signor Ladro, quando tornerai, bevi quanto whiskey vuoi...' Il viso di Helen era pallido. 'Non farlo, Judson,' urlò. 'È orribile, è un omicidio!'
pages.english_text: Then one by one he dropped the pills into the bottle and held it up to watch them dissolve. His wife looked at him in horror. 'What are they?' she asked him at last. 'Will they make the man sick?' 'Not only sick. They will kill him,' he answered with satisfaction. He closed the bottle and put it back on the shelf near the little whiskey-glass. He was pleased. Then he said: 'Now, Mr. Thief, when you come back, drink as much whiskey as you wish...' Helen's face was pale. 'Don't do it, Judson,' she cried. 'It's horrible, it's murder!'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Lo uccideranno,' rispose lui con soddisfazione.
translation.answer: 'They will kill him,' he answered with satisfaction.

comprehension.question: What effect did Judson say the pills would have?
comprehension.answer: They would kill the thief.

comprehension.question: How did Helen react?
comprehension.answer: She was horrified and called it murder.

pages.italian_text: 'La legge non lo chiama omicidio quando sparo a un ladro che sta entrando in casa mia con la forza.' 'Non farlo,' supplicò lei, 'la legge non punisce i furti con scasso con la morte, che diritto hai tu di farlo?' 'Quando si tratta di proteggere la mia proprietà, faccio le mie stesse leggi.' Helen cercò di convincerlo ma invano. Poi disse: 'Non voglio assistere a questo. Farò una passeggiata lungo la strada e vado a salutare tutti alla fattoria,' disse con calma. Aveva deciso di raccontare tutto alla moglie del custode. Qualcuno lo doveva sapere. 'Va bene, cara,' sorrise lui, 'e non preoccuparti del tuo povero piccolo ladruncolo. Nessuno si farà del male a meno che non irrompa in questa casa.'
pages.english_text: 'The law does not call it murder if I shoot a thief who is entering my house by force.' 'Don't do it,' she begged, 'the law does not punish burglary by death, what rights have you?' 'When it comes to protecting my property, I make my own laws.' Helen tried to convince him but in vain. Then she said: 'I don't want to watch this. I'll walk down the road and say good-bye at the farmhouse,' she said quietly. She had made up her mind to tell everything to caretaker's wife. Somebody had to know. 'All right, my dear,' he smiled, 'and don't worry about your poor little burglar. No one is going to be hurt unless he breaks in.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Quando si tratta di proteggere la mia proprietà, faccio le mie stesse leggi.'
translation.answer: When it comes to protecting my property, I make my own laws.

comprehension.question: What did Helen decide to do?
comprehension.answer: To go to the farmhouse and tell everything to the caretaker's wife.

comprehension.question: How did Judson respond to her plan?
comprehension.answer: He smiled and told her not to worry, saying no one would be hurt unless they broke in.

pages.italian_text: Helen camminò lungo la strada e Judson iniziò a chiudere la porta dell'armadio. Poi, improvvisamente si ricordò che non aveva messo in valigia i suoi stivali da caccia che si stavano asciugando fuori sul pesante tavolo in giardino. Quindi, lasciando la porta aperta, andò a prenderli. Ma mentre stava prendendo i suoi stivali, improvvisamente scivolò su un sasso e la sua testa colpì il massiccio tavolo mentre cadeva. Alcuni minuti più tardi sentì un forte braccio attorno a lui e la voce di Alec che diceva 'Va tutto bene, Signor Webb, non è stata una brutta caduta. Prenda questo - la farà sentire meglio.' Un piccolo bicchiere di whiskey fu premuto alle sue labbra. Semicosciente bevve.
pages.english_text: Helen went down the road and Judson started to close the closet door. Then he suddenly remembered that he had not packed his hunting boots that were drying outside on the heavy table in the garden. So, leaving the door open, he went to get them. But while he was taking his boots he suddenly slipped on a stone and his head struck the massive table as he fell. Several minutes later he felt a strong arm round him and Alec's voice saying 'It's all right, Mr. Webb, it was not a bad fall. Take this - it will make you feel better.' A small whiskey-glass was pressed to his lips. Half-conscious he drank.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Un piccolo bicchiere di whiskey fu premuto alle sue labbra.'
translation.answer: A small whiskey-glass was pressed to his lips.

comprehension.question: What happened to Judson when he went to get his boots?
comprehension.answer: He slipped on a stone and hit his head on the massive table.

comprehension.question: Who came to help him?
comprehension.answer: Alec.

comprehension.question: What did Alec give him to drink?
comprehension.answer: Whiskey from the same bottle.

overall_questions.question: Why did Judson Webb put poison in the whiskey bottle?
overall_questions.answer: To kill the person who had broken into his closet and stolen his whiskey the previous winter, and might do it again.

overall_questions.question: What was Helen's reaction when she saw the pills?
overall_questions.answer: She was horrified and called it murder.

overall_questions.question: What did Helen plan to do after leaving the house?
overall_questions.answer: To tell everything to the caretaker's wife.

overall_questions.question: What did Judson forget to pack?
overall_questions.answer: His hunting boots that were drying outside on the heavy table in the garden.

overall_questions.question: What happened when Judson went to get his boots?
overall_questions.answer: He slipped on a stone, fell, and hit his head on the table.

overall_questions.question: Who found Judson after his fall?
overall_questions.answer: Alec, the caretaker.

overall_questions.question: What did Alec give Judson to help him feel better?
overall_questions.answer: A small glass of whiskey (from the poisoned bottle).

overall_questions.question: Was Judson conscious when he drank the whiskey?
overall_questions.answer: He was half-conscious.

overall_questions.question: What is ironic about the ending?
overall_questions.answer: Judson himself drinks the poisoned whiskey he intended for the thief.

overall_questions.question: What does the title 'Ruthless' suggest about Judson?
overall_questions.answer: He is merciless and willing to kill to protect his property.

pages.italian_text: La ragazza vestita di grigio ritornò a quell'angolo tranquillo del piccolo parco all'inizio del crepuscolo. Si sedette su una panchina e iniziò a leggere un libro. Il suo vestito era molto semplice. Il suo viso era molto bello. Era venuta qui alla stessa ora il giorno precedente, e anche il giorno prima di quello, e c'era un giovane uomo che lo sapeva. Il giovane uomo vide la ragazza e si avvicinò. In quel momento, il libro le scivolò dalle dita e cadde a terra. Il giovane uomo raccolse il libro, lo ritornò gentilmente alla ragazza, dicendo alcune parole sul tempo, e rimase in piedi in attesa.
pages.english_text: The girl in grey came again to that quiet corner of the small park at the beginning of twilight. She sat down upon a bench and began to read a book. Her dress was very simple. Her face was very beautiful. She had come here at the same hour on the previous day, and on the day before that, and there was a young man who knew it. The young man saw the girl and came near. At that moment her book slipped from her fingers and fell on the ground. The young man picked up the book, returned it to the girl politely, saying a few words about the weather, and stood waiting.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Il suo vestito era molto semplice. Il suo viso era molto bello.'
translation.answer: Her dress was very simple. Her face was very beautiful.

comprehension.question: Where did the girl in grey sit?
comprehension.answer: On a bench in a quiet corner of a small park at twilight.

comprehension.question: What happened to her book?
comprehension.answer: It slipped from her fingers and fell on the ground.

pages.italian_text: La ragazza guardò il suo semplice cappotto e il suo viso comune. 'Puoi sederti, se vuoi,' disse lei. 'La luce non è adatta per leggere. Preferirei parlare.' 'Lo sai,' disse il giovane, 'che sei la ragazza più bella che io abbia mai visto. Ti ho visto qui ieri.' 'Chiunque tu sia,' disse la ragazza in un tono gelido, 'devi ricordare che sono una signora.' 'Chiedo scusa,' disse il giovane uomo. 'È stata colpa mia, sai – volevo dire, ci sono ragazze nel parco, lo sai – naturalmente che non lo sai, ma…' 'Cambiamo discorso. Certo che lo so. Ora, dimmi cosa pensi di queste persone che passano di qua. Dove stanno andando? Perché sembrano andare sempre di fretta? Sono felici?'
pages.english_text: The girl looked at his simple coat and his common face. 'You may sit down, if you like,' she said. 'The light is not good for reading. I would prefer to talk.' 'Do you know,' young man said, 'that you are the finest girl I have seen. I saw you here yesterday.' 'Whoever you are,' said the girl in an icy tone, 'you must remember that I am a lady.' 'I beg your pardon,' said the young man. 'It was my fault, you know – I mean, there are girls in the parks, you know – of course, you don't know, but…' 'Let's change the subject. Of course, I know. Now tell me what you think about these passing people. Where are they going? Why do they always seem in a hurry? Are they happy?'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'La luce non è adatta per leggere. Preferirei parlare.'
translation.answer: The light is not good for reading. I would prefer to talk.

comprehension.question: How did the girl react when the young man complimented her?
comprehension.answer: She reminded him in an icy tone that she was a lady.

comprehension.question: What question did she ask about the passing people?
comprehension.answer: Where they are going, why they are always in a hurry, and if they are happy.

pages.italian_text: Il giovane pensò per un attimo a come rispondere, ma la ragazza continuò. 'Io vengo in questo parco perché è solo qui che riesco a essere vicina alle masse di persone. Parlo con te perché voglio parlare con un uomo comune, non corrotto dal denaro. Oh! Tu non sai come sono stanca del denaro – denaro-denaro! E di tutti gli uomini che ho attorno. Sono stanca del divertimento, dei gioielli, di viaggiare.' 'Ho sempre avuto un'idea,' disse il giovane uomo, 'che il denaro deve essere una cosa molto bella.' 'Ebbene, quando hai i milioni! Autisti, cene, teatri, feste! Sono stanca di tutto questo!' disse la giovane ragazza.
pages.english_text: The young man thought for a moment how to respond, but the girl continued. 'I come to this park because it is only here that I can to be near the masses of people. I speak to you because I want to talk to a common man, unspoiled by money. Oh! You don't know how tired I am of money – money, money! And of the men who surround me. I am tired of pleasure, of jewels, of travel.' 'I always had an idea,' said the young man, 'that money must be a very good thing.' 'Well, when you have millions! Drivers, dinners, theaters, parties! I am tired of it!' said the young girl.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Non sai come sono stanca del denaro – denaro-denaro!'
translation.answer: You don't know how tired I am of money – money, money!

comprehension.question: Why does the girl come to this park?
comprehension.answer: Because only there can she be close to the masses of people, and she wants to talk to a common man unspoiled by money.

comprehension.question: What does the girl say she is tired of?
comprehension.answer: Money, the men who surround her, pleasure, jewels, and travel.

pages.italian_text: Il giovane la guardò con interesse. 'Mi è sempre piaciuto,' disse lui 'leggere e sentir parlare della vita delle persone ricche.' 'A volte,' continuò la ragazza, 'se mi innamorerò di un uomo, amerò un uomo semplice. – Qual è la tua professione?' 'Sono un uomo molto semplice. Ma spero di farmi strada nel mondo. Dicevi sul serio quando hai detto che potresti innamorarti di un uomo semplice?' 'Sì, davvero,' disse lei. 'Io lavoro in un ristorante,' disse lui. La ragazza si ritrasse un po'. 'Non come cameriere?' chiese lei. 'Sono un cassiere in quel ristorante che vedi laggiù con quella luminosa insegna elettrica: 'Ristorante'.'
pages.english_text: The young man looked at her with interest. 'I have always liked,' he said 'to read and to hear about the life of rich people.' 'Sometimes,' continued the girl, 'if I ever loved a man. I should love a simple man. – What is your profession?' 'I am a very simple man. But I hope to rise in the world. Did you really mean it when you said that you could love a simple man?' 'I really did,' she said. 'I work at a restaurant,' said he. The girl drew back a little. 'Not as a waiter?' she asked. 'I am a cashier in that restaurant you see over there with that brilliant electric sign: 'Restaurant'.'

vocabulary.question: What does 'farmaci strada nel mondo' mean?
vocabulary.answer: to make my way in the world

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Mi è sempre piaciuto leggere e sentir parlare della vita delle persone ricche.'
translation.answer: I have always liked to read and to hear about the life of rich people.

comprehension.question: What profession did the young man reveal?
comprehension.answer: He is a cashier at a restaurant.

comprehension.question: How did the girl react when he said he worked at a restaurant?
comprehension.answer: She drew back a little and asked if he was a waiter.

pages.italian_text: La ragazza guardò il suo orologio e si alzò. 'Perché non sei al lavoro ora?' chiese lei. 'Ho il turno di notte,' rispose il giovane uomo, 'ho ancora un'ora di tempo prima di iniziare il mio lavoro. Posso sperare di rivederti ancora?' 'Non lo so, forse. Ora devo andare. Ho una cena e un concerto stasera. Hai per caso notato un'auto bianca al cancello del parco quando sei arrivato?' 'Sì, l'ho vista,' disse il giovane. 'Arrivo sempre con quella. L'autista mi sta aspettando là. Buonasera.' 'Ma è quasi buio,' disse il giovane, 'e il parco è pieno di uomini maleducati. Posso accompagnarti all'auto?' 'No, tu rimarrai qui su questa panchina per altri dieci minuti dopo che me ne sarò andata.' E se ne andò.
pages.english_text: The girl looked at her watch and rose. 'Why are you not at work?' she asked. 'I am on the night shift,' replied the young man, 'it is still an hour till my work begins. May I hope to see you again?' 'I don't know, perhaps. I must go now. There is a dinner and concert tonight. May be you noticed a white automobile at the gate of the park when you came?' 'Yes, I did,' said the young man. 'I always come in it. The driver is waiting for me there. Good night.' 'But it's almost dark now,' said the young man, 'and the park is full of rude men. May I accompany you to the car?' 'No, you will remain on this bench for ten minutes after I have left.' And she went away.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Ho ancora un'ora di tempo prima di iniziare il mio lavoro.'
translation.answer: It is still an hour till my work begins.

comprehension.question: What did the girl say about a white automobile?
comprehension.answer: She always arrives in it, and the driver was waiting for her at the gate.

comprehension.question: Did she allow the young man to accompany her to the car?
comprehension.answer: No, she told him to remain on the bench for ten minutes after she left.

pages.italian_text: Il giovane osservò la sua elegante figura mentre camminava verso l'ingresso del parco. Poi si alzò e la seguì. Quando lei raggiunse il cancello del parco, girò la testa e guardò l'automobile, la passò e attraversò la strada ed entrò nel ristorante con la luminosa insegna elettrica: 'Ristorante'. Una ragazza dai capelli rossi lasciò la cassa, e la ragazza vestita di grigio prese il suo posto. Il giovane mise le mani in tasca e camminò lentamente giù per la strada. Poi entrò nell'automobile bianca e disse all'autista: 'Al club, Henry.'
pages.english_text: The young man looked at her elegant figure while she was walking to the entrance of the park. Then he rose and followed her. When she reached the park gate, she turned her head and looked at the car, then walked by it, crossed the street and entered the restaurant with the brilliant electric sign: 'Restaurant'. A red-haired girl left the cashier's desk, and the girl in grey took her place. The young man put his hands into his pockets and walked slowly down the street. Then he stepped into the white automobile and said to the driver: 'To the club, Henry.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Una ragazza dai capelli rossi lasciò la cassa, e la ragazza vestita di grigio prese il suo posto.'
translation.answer: A red-haired girl left the cashier's desk, and the girl in grey took her place.

comprehension.question: Where did the girl in grey actually go after leaving the park?
comprehension.answer: She entered the restaurant and took the place of the red-haired cashier.

comprehension.question: What did the young man do after watching her?
comprehension.answer: He stepped into the white automobile and told the driver to go to the club.

overall_questions.question: At what time of day does the story take place?
overall_questions.answer: At the beginning of twilight.

overall_questions.question: Why did the girl say she comes to the park?
overall_questions.answer: To be near the masses of people and to talk to a common man unspoiled by money.

overall_questions.question: What did the girl claim to be tired of?
overall_questions.answer: Money, the men who surround her, pleasure, jewels, and travel.

overall_questions.question: What profession did the young man say he had?
overall_questions.answer: Cashier at a restaurant.

overall_questions.question: What did the girl say about a white automobile?
overall_questions.answer: That she always arrives in it and the driver was waiting for her.

overall_questions.question: Did the young man accompany her to the car?
overall_questions.answer: No, she told him to stay on the bench for ten minutes after she left.

overall_questions.question: Where did the girl actually go after leaving the park?
overall_questions.answer: She crossed the street and entered the restaurant, taking the place of the red-haired cashier.

overall_questions.question: What did the young man do after she entered the restaurant?
overall_questions.answer: He walked slowly down the street, then stepped into the white automobile and told the driver to go to the club.

overall_questions.question: What is ironic about the girl's complaints about money and her identity?
overall_questions.answer: She pretends to be a rich lady tired of wealth, but she is actually a cashier at the restaurant.

overall_questions.question: What is ironic about the young man's identity?
overall_questions.answer: He pretends to be a simple cashier, but he actually owns the white automobile and goes to a club.

pages.italian_text: Una guardia arrivò al negozio di scarpe della prigione dove Jimmy Valentine stava lavorando e lo portò all'ufficio della prigione. Là, il direttore della prigione consegnò a Jimmy l'indulto, firmato la mattina stessa dal governatore. Jimmy lo prese con calma; era troppo stanco per mostrarsi emozionato. Era in prigione da quasi dieci mesi ed era stato condannato a quattro anni. È vero, pensava di rimanere in prigione solo tre mesi circa, al massimo. Aveva molti amici ed era sicuro che lo avrebbero aiutato. 'Ora, Valentine,' disse il direttore della prigione, 'Uscirai domani mattina. Non sei un cattivo ragazzo, per niente. Smettila di scassinare casseforti e fai l'onesto.'
pages.english_text: A guard came to the prison shoe shop where Jimmy Valentine was working and took him to the prison office. There the warden handed Jimmy his pardon, which has been signed that morning by the governor. Jimmy took it quietly; he was too tired to show excitement. He had been in prison nearly ten months and he had been sentenced to four years. True, he had expected to stay only about three months, at the longest. He had a lot of friends and he was sure they would help him. 'Now, Valentine,' said the warden, 'You'll get out in the morning. You're not a bad fellow really. Stop breaking open safes and be honest.'

vocabulary.question: What does 'scassinare' mean?
vocabulary.answer: to break open, to pick (a lock)

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Smettila di scassinare casseforti e fai l'onesto.'
translation.answer: Stop breaking open safes and be honest.

comprehension.question: How long had Jimmy been in prison?
comprehension.answer: Nearly ten months.

comprehension.question: What was Jimmy's original sentence?
comprehension.answer: Four years.

pages.italian_text: 'Io?' disse Jimmy, sorpreso. 'Perché, non ho mai scassinato una cassaforte in tutta la mia vita.' 'Certo che no' rise il direttore. 'E che ne dici del lavoro a Springfield? Vuoi dirmi che non ne hai preso parte?' 'Io?' disse Jimmy ancora più sorpreso. 'Direttore, non sono mai stato a Springfield in tutta la mia vita!' 'Riportalo al suo posto,' disse il direttore della prigione alla guardia sorridente, 'e dagli dei vestiti. Domani liberalo alle sette e portalo nell'ufficio. Faresti meglio a ripensare al mio consiglio, Valentine.' Alle sette e un quarto del mattino dopo, Jimmy si trovava nell'ufficio del direttore della prigione. Indossava un abito della taglia sbagliata e le scarpe economiche che lo stato dava ai detenuti, quando venivano liberati.
pages.english_text: 'Me?' said Jimmy, in surprise. 'Why, I've never broken open a safe in my life.' 'Of course not' laughed the warden. 'And what about that Springfield job? Do you mean to say you didn't take part in it?' 'Me?' said Jimmy even more surprised. 'Warden, I've never been to Springfield in my life!' 'Take him back,' the warden said to the guard smiling, 'and give him some clothes. Tomorrow unlock him at seven and bring him to the office. You better think over my advice, Valentine.' At a quarter past seven the next morning Jimmy stood in the warden's office. He wore a badly fitting suit and the cheap shoes that the state gives to prisoners, when they are set free.

vocabulary.question: What does 'un abito della taglia sbagliata' mean?
vocabulary.answer: a suit of the wrong size

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Non sono mai stato a Springfield in tutta la mia vita!'
translation.answer: I've never been to Springfield in my life!

comprehension.question: What did Jimmy claim about the Springfield job?
comprehension.answer: He had never been to Springfield and did not take part in it.

comprehension.question: What did the state give Jimmy when he was released?
comprehension.answer: A badly fitting suit and cheap shoes.

pages.italian_text: L'impiegato gli consegnò un biglietto ferroviario e una banconota da cinque dollari con cui avrebbe dovuto iniziare una nuova, onesta vita. Il direttore della prigione gli diede un sigaro, e si strinsero la mano. Valentine, 9762, era registrato nei registri 'Graziato dal Governatore', e il Signor James Valentine uscì alla luce del sole. Senza prestare attenzione al canto degli uccelli, agli alberi verdi e al profumo dei fiori, Jimmy andò dritto a un ristorante. Lì mangiò un arrosto di pollo e bevve una bottiglia di vino bianco – un pasto che non aveva gustato per molto tempo. Poi prese un treno e andò in una città vicino al confine.
pages.english_text: The clerk handed him a railroad ticket and a five-dollar bill with which he was supposed to start a new, honest life. The warden gave him a cigar, and they shook hands. Valentine, 9762, was registered in the books 'Pardoned by Governor', and Mr. James Valentine walked out into the sunshine. Paying no attention to the song of the birds, the green trees, and a smell of the flowers. Jimmy went straight to a restaurant. There he ate a roasted chicken and drank a bottle of white wine – a meal he had not tasted for a long time. Then he took a train and went to a town near the border.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Mangiò un arrosto di pollo e bevve una bottiglia di vino bianco.'
translation.answer: He ate a roasted chicken and drank a bottle of white wine.

comprehension.question: What did the clerk give Jimmy?
comprehension.answer: A railroad ticket and a five-dollar bill.

comprehension.question: Where did Jimmy go first after leaving prison?
comprehension.answer: To a restaurant.

pages.italian_text: Una settimana dopo il rilascio di Valentine, 9762, ci fu un'altra cassaforte scassinata a Richmond, Indiana. Furono rubati solo ottocento dollari. Due settimane dopo, venne aperta un'altra cassaforte e sparirono millecinquecento dollari; titoli e argento rimasero intatti. I detective iniziarono a interessarsi al caso. Alcuni giorni dopo la Jefferson City Bank venne rapinata e vennero prese banconote che ammontavano a cinque mila dollari. La somma presa era troppo alta ora ed era il momento che un detective noto come Ben Price iniziasse a investigare. Ben Price investigò le scene delle rapine e notò una somiglianza sorprendente nei metodi dei furti, e più tardi lo sentirono dire: 'Questo è tutto lavoro di Jimmy Valentine. Ha ripreso gli affari. Solo lui possiede strumenti così precisi per aprire qualsiasi cassaforte senza lasciare nemmeno la minima traccia. Sì, è il Signor Valentine.'
pages.english_text: A week after Valentine's release, there was another safe burglary in Richmond, Indiana. Only eight hundred dollars were stolen. Two weeks later, another safe was opened and fifteen hundred dollars disappeared; securities and silver remained intact. That began to interest the detectives. A few days later the Jefferson City Bank was robbed and banknotes amounting to five thousand dollars were taken. The amount taken was too high now and it was time for so well-known a detective as Ben Price to begin investigation. Ben Price investigated the scenes of the robberies and noticed a striking similarity in the methods of the burglaries and later he was heard to say: 'That's all Jimmy Valentine's work. He's resumed business. Only he has those fine tools that can open any safe without leaving the slightest trace. Yes, it is Mr. Valentine.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Solo lui possiede strumenti così precisi per aprire qualsiasi cassaforte senza lasciare nemmeno la minima traccia.'
translation.answer: Only he has those fine tools that can open any safe without leaving the slightest trace.

comprehension.question: How much money was stolen from the Jefferson City Bank?
comprehension.answer: Five thousand dollars.

comprehension.question: Who was the famous detective that began investigating?
comprehension.answer: Ben Price.

pages.italian_text: Un pomeriggio, Jimmy Valentine e la sua valigia uscirono da un treno a Elmore, un piccolo paese in Arkansas. Jimmy, che sembrava come uno studente appena ritornato a casa dal college, uscì dalla stazione e si diresse verso l'hotel. Una giovane donna attraversò la strada, gli passò davanti all'angolo ed entrò in una porta, sopra cui c'era l'insegna 'The Elmore Bank.' Jimmy Valentine la guardò negli occhi, dimenticò cos'era, e diventò un altro uomo. Lei abbassò gli occhi e arrossì leggermente. Un giovane uomo con lo stile e l'aspetto di Jimmy scarseggiavano ad Elmore.
pages.english_text: One afternoon Jimmy Valentine and his suitcase climbed out of a train in Elmore, a little town in Arkansas. Jimmy, looking like a student who had just come home from college, walked out of the station and went toward the hotel. A young lady crossed the street, passed him at the corner and entered a door over which was the sign 'The Elmore Bank.' Jimmy Valentine looked into her eyes, forgot what he was, and became another man. She lowered her eyes and blushed slightly. Young man of Jimmy's style and looks were scarce in Elmore.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Jimmy Valentine la guardò negli occhi, dimenticò cos'era, e diventò un altro uomo.'
translation.answer: Jimmy Valentine looked into her eyes, forgot what he was, and became another man.

comprehension.question: Where did Jimmy go after getting off the train?
comprehension.answer: Elmore, a little town in Arkansas.

comprehension.question: Who did Jimmy see entering the bank?
comprehension.answer: A young lady (Annabel Adams).

pages.italian_text: Jimmy chiamò un ragazzo che si trovava sui gradini della banca, come se fosse stato uno degli azionisti, e iniziò a fargli domande sulla città, dandogli ogni tanto qualche centesimo. Dopo un po' di tempo, la giovane donna uscì, passò di nuovo davanti a Jimmy fingendo di non vederlo, e proseguì per la sua strada. 'Quella giovane donna non è la Signorina Polly Simpson?' chiese Jimmy astutamente. 'No,' disse il ragazzo. 'È Annabel Adams. Suo padre possiede questa banca.' Jimmy si diresse verso il Planters' Hotel, si registrò come Ralph D. Spencer, e prenotò una stanza. Si appoggiò sul bancone e dichiarò le sue intenzioni all'impiegato. Disse che era venuto a Elmore per iniziare un'attività.
pages.english_text: Jimmy called a boy who was standing on the steps of the bank as if he were one of the stockholders, and began to ask him questions about the town, giving the boy dimes from time to time. After a short while the young lady came out, passed Jimmy again, pretending not to see him, and went on her way. 'Isn't that young lady Miss Polly Simpson?' asked Jimmy shrewdly. 'No,' said the boy. 'She is Annabel Adams. Her father owns this bank.' Jimmy went to the Planters' Hotel, registered as Ralph D. Spencer, and reserved a room. He leaned on the desk and stated his intentions to the clerk. He said he had come to Elmore to start business.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Si registrò come Ralph D. Spencer.'
translation.answer: He registered as Ralph D. Spencer.

comprehension.question: What name did Jimmy register under at the hotel?
comprehension.answer: Ralph D. Spencer.

comprehension.question: What did Jimmy say he had come to Elmore to do?
comprehension.answer: To start a business.

pages.italian_text: Com'era il settore delle calzature in paese? Valeva la pena aprire un negozio di scarpe? L'impiegato rimase colpito dall'abbigliamento e dalle maniere di Jimmy ed era pronto a dare al giovane uomo tutte le informazioni che desiderava. Sì, valeva la pena investire nel settore delle calzature, pensò. Non c'era un negozio di scarpe in paese. L'attività sembrava una buona idea sotto tutti i punti di vista. 'Mi auguro, Signor Spencer, che lei decida di rimanere ad Elmore. Troverà che è una piacevole città in cui vivere, e gli abitanti sono molto cordiali,' continuò l'impiegato. Il Signor Spencer disse che sarebbe rimasto per qualche giorno per considerare la situazione. L'impiegato voleva chiamare il ragazzo per portare la valigia al piano superiore, ma il Signor Spencer disse che avrebbe portato la valigia da sé; era piuttosto pesante.
pages.english_text: How was the shoe business now in the town? Was it worthwhile opening a shoestore? The clerk was impressed by the clothes and manner of Jimmy and he was ready to give the young man any information he desired. Yes, it was worthwhile investing in the shoe business, he thought. There wasn't a shoe store in the town. The business appeared to be a good idea from any point of view. 'I hope, Mr. Spencer, you'll decide to stay in Elmore. You'll find it a pleasant town to live in, and the people are very nice,' continued the clerk. Mr. Spencer said that he would stay for a few days and consider the situation. The clerk wanted to call the boy to carry up the suitcase, but Mr. Spencer said that he would carry his suitcase himself; it was rather heavy.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'La valigia era piuttosto pesante.'
translation.answer: The suitcase was rather heavy.

comprehension.question: Why did the clerk think opening a shoe store was a good idea?
comprehension.answer: Because there was no shoe store in town.

comprehension.question: Why did Jimmy insist on carrying his own suitcase?
comprehension.answer: Because it was rather heavy (it contained his burglar tools).

pages.italian_text: Il Signor Ralph Spencer, la fenice sorta dalle ceneri di Jimmy Valentine - ceneri rimaste dalla fiamma di un improvviso attacco d'amore - rimase ad Elmore e prosperò. Aprì un negozio di scarpe e fece grandi profitti. Sotto tutti gli aspetti ebbe successo. Aveva molto successo tra le persone importanti e aveva molti amici. Riuscì pure a soddisfare un desiderio del suo cuore. Incontrò la Signorina Annabel Adams e se ne innamorò profondamente. Dopo un anno la situazione del Signor Ralph Spencer era questa: si era guadagnato il rispetto di gran parte degli abitanti del luogo, la sua attività di calzature era prospera, e si sarebbe sposato con Annabel tra due settimane.
pages.english_text: Mr. Ralph Spencer, the phoenix that arose from Jimmy Valentine's ashes - ashes left by the flame of a sudden attack of love - remained in Elmore and prospered. He opened a shoe store and was making large profits. In all other respects he was also a success. He was popular with many important people and had many friends. And he fulfilled the wish of his heart. He met Miss Annabel Adams, and fell deeply in love with her. After a year the situation of Mr. Ralph Spencer was this: he had won the respect of most the inhabitants of the place, his shoe store was prospering, and he and Annabel were to be married in two weeks.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Riuscì pure a soddisfare un desiderio del suo cuore.'
translation.answer: He also managed to fulfill a wish of his heart.

comprehension.question: How did Jimmy (as Ralph Spencer) succeed in Elmore?
comprehension.answer: He opened a shoe store, made large profits, became popular, and fell in love with Annabel Adams.

comprehension.question: When was the wedding planned?
comprehension.answer: In two weeks.

pages.italian_text: Un giorno Jimmy si sedette in camera sua e scrisse questa lettera che spedì all'indirizzo di uno dei suoi vecchi amici: 'Carlo Vecchio Amico, voglio che tu venga al Brown's Cafe, a Little Rock, il prossimo mercoledì alle ore nove di sera. Voglio che tu faccia qualcosa per me. E, vorrei anche darti in dono i miei strumenti. Lo so che sarai contento di averli - non riusciresti ad avere un tale set nemmeno per mille dollari. Billy, ho rinunciato alla mia vecchia attività un anno fa. Mi guadagno da vivere in modo onesto ora e tra due settimane mi sposerò con la ragazza più bella sulla terra. Ora, non toccherei un dollaro di un'altra persona nemmeno per un milione. Dopo il mio matrimonio, ho intenzione di vendere il mio negozio di scarpe e di spostarmi verso ovest, dove non correrò il rischio di incontrare persone che mi conoscevano prima. Ti dirò Billy, lei è un angelo. Lei crede in me e non potrei mai fare un'altra cosa sbagliata per niente al mondo. Per favore, vieni da Brown's, perché devo vederti. Porterò con me gli strumenti. Il tuo vecchio amico, Jimmy.'
pages.english_text: One day Jimmy sat down in his room and wrote this letter which he sent to the address of one of his old friends: 'Dear Old Chap, I want you to be at Brown's Café, in Little Rock, next Wednesday at nine o'clock in the evening. I want you to do something for me. And, also, I want to make you a present of my tools. I know you'll be glad to have them - you couldn't get such a set for a thousand dollars. Billy, I gave up the old business a year ago. I am making an honest living now and in two weeks I'm going to marry the finest girl on earth. I wouldn't touch a dollar of another man's money now for a million. After I get married I'm going to sell my shoe store and move west, where there won't be a danger of meeting people who knew me before. I tell you, Billy, she's an angel. She believes in me and I would never do another wrong thing for the whole world. Do come to Brown's, because I must see you. I'll bring the tools with me. Your old friend, Jimmy.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Non toccherei un dollaro di un'altra persona nemmeno per un milione.'
translation.answer: I wouldn't touch a dollar of another man's money now for a million.

comprehension.question: Why did Jimmy write to his old friend Billy?
comprehension.answer: To give him his burglar tools and say goodbye to his old life.

comprehension.question: What did Jimmy plan to do after his wedding?
comprehension.answer: Sell his shoe store and move west to avoid people who knew him before.

pages.italian_text: Il lunedì sera, dopo che Jimmy scrisse questa lettera, Ben Price, il detective arrivò ad Elmore. Camminò per il paese finché scoprì quello che voleva sapere. Dalla farmacia che si trovava sul lato opposto della strada del negozio di scarpe di Spencer, osservò attentamente Ralph D. Spencer. 'Ti sposerai con la figlia del banchiere, vero Jimmy?' disse Ben tra sé, piano. 'Beh, non lo so!' Il mattino seguente, Jimmy fece colazione a casa di Adams. Oggi doveva andare a Little Rock a ordinare il suo abito da sposo e acquistare qualcosa di carino per Annabel. Questa sarebbe stata la prima volta che lasciava il paese dopo il suo arrivo ad Elmore.
pages.english_text: On the Monday night after Jimmy wrote this letter, Ben Price, the detective arrived in Elmore. He walked around town until he found what he wanted to know. From the pharmacy across the street from Spencer's shoe store he took a good look at Ralph D. Spencer. 'Going to marry the banker's daughter, are you, Jimmy?' said Ben to himself, softly. 'Well, I don't know!' The next morning Jimmy took breakfast at the Adams' house. He was going to Little Rock that day to order his wedding suit and buy something nice for Annabel. That would be the first time he had left town since he came to Elmore.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Questa sarebbe stata la prima volta che lasciava il paese dopo il suo arrivo ad Elmore.'
translation.answer: That would be the first time he had left town since he came to Elmore.

comprehension.question: Who came to Elmore looking for Jimmy?
comprehension.answer: Detective Ben Price.

comprehension.question: Where was Jimmy going that day?
comprehension.answer: To Little Rock to order his wedding suit and buy something for Annabel.

pages.italian_text: Dopo colazione, l'intera famiglia andò a fare una lunga passeggiata: Il Signor Adams, Annabel, Jimmy e la sorella sposata di Annabel con le sue due piccole figlie, di cinque e nove anni. Passarono per l'hotel dove Jimmy ancora soggiornava, e lui corse su nella sua stanza a prendere la sua valigia. Poi proseguirono verso la banca. Alla banca si trovava il cavallo di Jimmy, il calesse e il cocchiere che lo avrebbe portato alla stazione ferroviaria. Entrarono tutti in banca - incluso Jimmy. Gli impiegati erano felici di essere salutati da quel giovane uomo di bell'aspetto e dai modi piacevoli che stava per sposare la Signorina Annabel. Jimmy appoggiò a terra la valigia.
pages.english_text: After breakfast the whole family went for a walk together: Mr. Adams, Annabel, Jimmy, and Annabel's married sister with her two little girls, ages five and nine. They passed by the hotel where Jimmy still stayed, and he ran up to his room and brought his suitcase. Then they went on to the bank. There at the bank stood Jimmy's horse and buggy and the coachman who was going to drive him to a railroad station. All went inside the bank - Jimmy included. The clerks were pleased to be greeted by the good-looking, pleasant young man who was going to marry Miss Annabel. Jimmy put his suitcase down.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Jimmy appoggiò a terra la valigia.'
translation.answer: Jimmy put his suitcase down.

comprehension.question: Who accompanied the family on the walk?
comprehension.answer: Mr. Adams, Annabel, Jimmy, Annabel's sister, and her two little girls.

comprehension.question: Where did Jimmy pick up his suitcase?
comprehension.answer: From his room at the hotel.

pages.italian_text: Annabel, che aveva il cuore che batteva di felicità e gioventù, indossò il cappello di Jimmy, e prese la valigia. 'Non sono carina?' disse Annabel. 'Oh, mio caro Ralph, ma quanto è pesante. Pesa come se fosse piena di lingotti d'oro.' 'Ci sono molte calzascarpe in nichel qui,' disse Jimmy freddamente, 'che devo ritornare. Ho deciso di portarle io stesso per evitare delle spese inutili. Sto diventando terribilmente economico.' La Elmore Bank aveva appena installato una nuova cassaforte e una camera blindata. Il Signor Adams era molto fiero della camera blindata e insistette che tutti gli dessero un'occhiata. La camera blindata era piccola, ma aveva una porta nuova e moderna. Era fissata con tre bulloni di acciaio e aveva un sistema di sicurezza per aprirla a ore prestabilite.
pages.english_text: Annabel whose heart was beating with happiness and youth, put on Jimmy's hat, and picked up the suitcase. 'Don't I look nice?' said Annabel. 'Oh, my, Ralph, how heavy it is! It weighs as much as if it were full of gold bricks.' 'There are a lot of nickel shoehorns there,' said Jimmy coolly, 'that I'm going to return. I decided to take them myself so that to avoid unnecessary expenses. I'm getting awfully economical.' The Elmore Bank had just put in a new safe and vault. Mr. Adams was very proud of the vault and insisted that everyone should take a look at it. The vault was a small one, but it had a new modern door. It fastened with three steel bolts and had a security system to open it at predetermined hours.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Pesa come se fosse piena di lingotti d'oro.'
translation.answer: It weighs as much as if it were full of gold bricks.

comprehension.question: What did Jimmy claim was in the heavy suitcase?
comprehension.answer: Nickel shoehorns that he was going to return.

comprehension.question: What new feature had the Elmore Bank recently installed?
comprehension.answer: A new safe and vault with a modern door and security system.

pages.italian_text: Il Signor Adams spiegò con entusiasmo al Signor Spencer come funzionava, il quale, tuttavia, non sembrava mostrare grande interesse. Le due bambine May e Agatha, erano entusiaste nel vedere il metallo luccicante e quella divertente sveglia. Mentre erano così occupati, Ben Price, il detective, entrò in banca e si appoggiò sul gomito, guardando casualmente tra la ringhiera. Disse al cassiere che non voleva niente; stava solo aspettando un uomo che conosceva. Improvvisamente, si sentì un terribile urlo dalle donne. Non vista dagli adulti, May, la bambina di nove anni, mentre giocava con la sorella, chiuse Agatha nella camera blindata. Il vecchio banchiere afferrò la maniglia e per un momento provò a tirarla. 'La porta non può essere aperta,' urlò. 'La sveglia non è stata caricata.'
pages.english_text: Mr. Adams enthusiastically explained how it works to Mr. Spencer, who, however, didn't seem to take a great interest in it. The two children May and Agatha, were delighted to see the shining metal and the funny clock. While they were thus engaged, Ben Price, the detective, walked into the bank and leaned on his elbow, looking casually inside between the railings. He told the cashier that he didn't want anything; he was just waiting for a man he knew. Suddenly there was a terrible scream from the women. Unseen by the elders, May, the nine-year-old girl, while playing with her sister, had shut her in the vault. The old banker grabbed the handle and tugged at it for a moment. 'The door can't be opened,' he cried out. 'The clock has not been wound.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'La sveglia non è stata caricata.'
translation.answer: The clock has not been wound.

comprehension.question: What happened to Agatha?
comprehension.answer: May accidentally shut her inside the vault.

comprehension.question: Why couldn't the vault door be opened?
comprehension.answer: Because the clock had not been wound.

pages.italian_text: La madre di Agatha urlò ancora, istericamente. 'Silenzio!' disse il Signor Adams, alzando la mano tremante. 'Fate silenzio per un momento. Agatha!' chiamò più forte che poté. 'Ascoltami...' Nell'attimo di silenzio udirono il debole suono della bambina che piangeva nella buia camera blindata. 'Tesoro mio!' urlò la madre. 'Morirà di spavento! Aprite la porta! Oh, scassatela! Voi uomini non potete fare qualcosa?' 'Non c'è alcun uomo fino a Little Rock che sia in grado di aprire questa porta,' disse il Signor Adams con voce tremante. 'Oh, Santo Cielo! Spencer, cosa possiamo fare? Quella bambina – non può rimanere là dentro a lungo. Non c'è abbastanza ossigeno, e inoltre, impazzirà dalla paura.' La madre di Agatha si mise a battere selvaggiamente con le mani sulla porta della camera blindata. Qualcuno suggerì la dinamite.
pages.english_text: Agatha's mother screamed again, hysterically. 'Hush!' said Mr. Adams, raising his trembling hand. 'All be quiet for a moment. Agatha!' he called as loudly as he could. 'Listen to me...' During the following silence they could hear the faint sound of the child crying in the dark vault. 'My darling!' cried the mother. 'She will die of fright! Open the door! Oh, break it open! Can't you men do something?' 'There isn't a man nearer than Little Rock who can open that door,' said Mr. Adams in a trembling voice. 'My God! Spencer, what shall we do? That child – she can't stand it long in there. There isn't enough air, and, besides she'll go mad from fright.' Agatha's mother beat the door of the vault wildly with her hands. Somebody suggested dynamite.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Non c'è abbastanza ossigeno.'
translation.answer: There isn't enough air.

comprehension.question: What did the mother fear would happen to Agatha?
comprehension.answer: She would die of fright, run out of air, or go mad from fear.

comprehension.question: What did someone suggest to open the vault?
comprehension.answer: Dynamite.

pages.italian_text: Annabel si rivolse a Jimmy, i suoi grandi occhi pieni di orrore, ma non ancora disperati. Per una donna, nulla sembra essere impossibile dall'uomo che ama. 'Non puoi fare qualcosa, Ralph – proverai, vero?' Lui la guardò con uno strano, dolce sorriso sulle labbra e negli occhi. 'Annabel,' disse lui, 'dammi quella rosa che hai addosso, va bene?' Incredula di aver capito correttamente cosa gli aveva chiesto, sganciò il fiore dal suo vestito e lo diede a Jimmy. Lo mise nella tasca del suo gilet, si tolse il cappotto e tirò su le maniche della camicia. Con questo atto, Ralph D. Spencer sparì e Jimmy Valentine prese il suo posto. 'Allontanatevi dalla porta, tutti,' ordinò bruscamente. Mise la sua valigia sopra al tavolo e la aprì. Da quel momento in poi, sembrava inconsapevole della presenza degli altri.
pages.english_text: Annabel turned to Jimmy, her large eyes full of horror, but not yet despairing. To a woman nothing seems impossible to the powers of the man she loves. 'Can't you do something, Ralph – try, won't you?' He looked at her with a strange, soft smile on his lips and in his eyes. 'Annabel,' he said, 'give me that rose you are wearing, will you?' Hardly believing that she heard him correctly she unpinned the flower from her dress, and gave it to Jimmy. He put it into his vest-pocket, threw off his coat and pulled his shirt sleeves. With that act Ralph D. Spencer passed away and Jimmy Valentine took his place. 'Get away from the door, all of you,' he commanded, shortly. He put his suitcase on the table and opened it. From that moment on he seemed to be unaware of the presence of anyone else.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Ralph D. Spencer sparì e Jimmy Valentine prese il suo posto.'
translation.answer: Ralph D. Spencer passed away and Jimmy Valentine took his place.

comprehension.question: What did Jimmy ask Annabel for?
comprehension.answer: The rose she was wearing.

comprehension.question: What did Jimmy do with his suitcase?
comprehension.answer: He opened it on the table.

pages.italian_text: Tirò fuori rapidamente e con cura strani strumenti, fischiettando tra sé come faceva sempre quando lavorava. Nel profondo silenzio gli altri lo osservarono a bocca aperta. In dieci minuti - battendo il suo stesso record di scassi - aprì la porta. La madre di Agatha si precipitò nella camera blindata e prese la bambina, che era molto debole ma salva. Jimmy Valentine si rimise il cappotto e camminò verso la porta principale. Mentre se ne andava gli sembrò di sentire una voce che una volta conosceva, chiamare 'Ralph'. Ma non si fermò nemmeno un istante. Alla porta un grosso uomo gli bloccò la strada. 'Ciao Ben!' disse Jimmy, ancora con il suo strano sorriso. 'Finalmente sei qui, vero? Bene, andiamo. Credo non abbia molta importanza ora.' Poi, Ben Price si comportò in modo strano. 'Penso che lei si stia sbagliando, Signor Spencer,' disse lui, 'Non mi sembra di riconoscerla. Il suo calesse la sta aspettando, vero?' E Ben Price si girò e camminò giù per la strada.
pages.english_text: He took out strange instruments quickly and orderly, whistling to himself as he always did when he was at work. In the deep silence the others watched him dumbfounded. In ten minutes - breaking his own burglarious record - he opened the door. Agatha's mother rushed into the vault and took the child, who was very weak, but safe. Jimmy Valentine put on his coat and walked towards the front door. As he went, it seemed to him he heard a voice that he once knew call 'Ralph'. But he didn't stop for an instant. At the door a big man stood in his way. 'Hello, Ben!' said Jimmy, still with his strange smile. 'At last you're here, are you? Well, let's go. I don't think it matters much now.' And then Ben Price acted rather strangely. 'I guess you're mistaken, Mr. Spencer,' he said, 'I don't believe I recognize you. Your buggy's waiting for you, isn't it?' And Ben Price turned and walked down the street.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'In dieci minuti - battendo il suo stesso record di scassi - aprì la porta.'
translation.answer: In ten minutes - breaking his own burglarious record - he opened the door.

comprehension.question: How long did it take Jimmy to open the vault?
comprehension.answer: Ten minutes.

comprehension.question: Why did Ben Price pretend not to recognize Jimmy?
comprehension.answer: He allowed Jimmy to go free because he had reformed and saved a child's life.

overall_questions.question: Why was Jimmy Valentine in prison?
overall_questions.answer: For breaking open safes (burglary).

overall_questions.question: How long was his sentence?
overall_questions.answer: Four years, but he was pardoned after ten months.

overall_questions.question: What did Jimmy do immediately after his release?
overall_questions.answer: He went to a restaurant, ate a roasted chicken, drank white wine, then took a train to a town near the border.

overall_questions.question: What series of crimes caught the attention of detective Ben Price?
overall_questions.answer: Safe burglaries in Richmond, Indiana, and the Jefferson City Bank robbery.

overall_questions.question: What new identity did Jimmy assume in Elmore?
overall_questions.answer: Ralph D. Spencer.

overall_questions.question: What business did he start in Elmore?
overall_questions.answer: A shoe store.
