overall_questions.question: Who was Annabel Adams?
overall_questions.answer: The daughter of the owner of the Elmore Bank, and the woman Jimmy fell in love with.

overall_questions.question: What happened to Agatha in the bank?
overall_questions.answer: Her sister May accidentally shut her inside the new vault.

overall_questions.question: How did Jimmy save Agatha?
overall_questions.answer: He used his burglar tools to open the vault in ten minutes.

overall_questions.question: Why did Ben Price let Jimmy go?
overall_questions.answer: Because Jimmy had reformed, was living honestly, and had just saved a child's life.

pages.italian_text: Il Signor Smith era seduto nella sua biblioteca e stava leggendo il giornale. Qualcuno bussò alla porta e Koppel il suo servitore entrò. 'Desidera un succo d'arancia, signore?' disse gentilmente il servitore al suo padrone. 'No,' rispose il Signor Smith. 'Ma le farà bene, signore. Sono gli ordini del medico.' 'No.' Koppel sentì suonare il campanello della porta e fu contento di lasciare la stanza. Nell'atrio al piano di sotto trovò il dottore. 'Non posso fare niente con il vecchio,' disse al dottore. 'Non vuole bere il suo succo. Non vuole che gli legga qualcosa. Odia la radio. Non gli piace niente.'
pages.english_text: Mr. Smith was sitting in his library room reading the newspaper. There was a knock at the door and his servant Koppel came in. 'Will you take your orange juice, sir?' the servant said gently to his master. 'No,' answered Mr. Smith. 'But it's good for you, sir. It's the doctor's orders.' 'No.' Koppel heard the front door bell and was glad to leave the room. He found the doctor in the hall downstairs. 'I can't do anything with the old man,' he said to the doctor. 'He doesn't want to take his juice. He doesn't want me to read to him. He hates the radio. He doesn't like anything.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Non vuole bere il suo succo.'
translation.answer: He doesn't want to take his juice.

comprehension.question: What was Mr. Smith doing when Koppel entered?
comprehension.answer: Reading the newspaper in his library.

comprehension.question: What did the doctor want Mr. Smith to take?
comprehension.answer: Orange juice.

pages.italian_text: Il Dottor Jones prese l'informazione con la sua solita calma professionale. Aveva pensato molto al suo paziente dalla sua ultima visita. Questo non era un caso ordinario. Il vecchio signore era piuttosto in buona forma per un uomo di settantasei anni. Ma si doveva fare qualcosa con lui. Era necessario trattenerlo dal fare acquisti. Il fatto era che il vecchio soffriva notevolmente per i suoi acquisti. Il suo ultimo attacco di cuore avvenne dopo il disastroso acquisto di una ferrovia in uno degli Stati Occidentali. Un altro attacco era il risultato della bancarotta di alcuni negozi di generi alimentari, acquistati ad un prezzo molto alto. Tutti questi acquisti dovevano essere liquidati con grande sacrificio sia per la sua salute che per il portafoglio.
pages.english_text: Doctor Jones received the information with his usual professional calm. He had thought a lot about his patient since his last visit. This was not an ordinary case. The old gentleman was in rather good shape for a man of seventy six. But something had to be done with him. He needed to be kept from buying things. The fact is that the old man suffered considerably from his purchases. His latest heart attack happened after his disastrous purchase of a railroad in one of the Western States. Another attack was the result of the bankruptcy of some grocery shops, which he had bought at a very high price. All of these purchases had to be liquidated at a great sacrifice both to his health and to his pocketbook.

vocabulary.question: What does 'trattenerlo' mean?
vocabulary.answer: to keep him from, to restrain him

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Il vecchio signore era piuttosto in buona forma per un uomo di settantasei anni.'
translation.answer: The old gentleman was in rather good shape for a man of seventy-six.

comprehension.question: What caused Mr. Smith's heart attacks?
comprehension.answer: Disastrous purchases (a railroad and grocery shops that went bankrupt).

comprehension.question: What problem did the doctor want to solve?
comprehension.answer: To keep Mr. Smith from buying things that harmed his health and finances.

pages.italian_text: Il dottore ragionò su tutto questo ancora una volta prima di entrare nella stanza del suo paziente. Si avvicinò al Signor Smith sorridente. 'Bene, come sta oggi questo giovane uomo?' 'Umph,' udì dalla figura seduta nella poltrona. 'Mi hanno detto che non obbedisce agli ordini,' continuò il dottore. 'Chi può darmi ordini alla mia età?' Il dottore prese una sedia e si sedette vicino al vecchio. 'Ho una proposta da farle,' disse con calma. Il vecchio lo guardò con sospetto sopra gli occhiali. 'Cos'è questo? Altre medicine, altri giri in automobile, altre sciocchezze per tenermi lontano dagli affari?' 'Le piacerebbe studiare arte?' 'Sciocchezze.' 'Non intendo seriamente, solo per divertimento.' 'Sciocchezze.'
pages.english_text: The doctor once again reflected on all this before he entered his patient's room. He approached Mr. Smith smiling. 'Well, how's the young man today?' 'Umph,' came from the figure in the armchair. 'I hear you don't obey orders,' went on the doctor. 'Who can give me orders at my age?' The doctor took a chair and sat down close to the old man. 'I have a proposition for you,' he said quietly. The old man looked at him suspiciously over his glasses. 'What is it? More medicine, more automobile rides, more nonsense to keep me away from business?' 'How would you like to study art?' 'Nonsense.' 'I don't mean seriously, just for fun.' 'Nonsense.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Chi può darmi ordini alla mia età?'
translation.answer: Who can give me orders at my age?

comprehension.question: What did the doctor propose to Mr. Smith?
comprehension.answer: To study art just for fun.

comprehension.question: How did Mr. Smith initially react to the proposal?
comprehension.answer: He called it nonsense.

pages.italian_text: 'Va bene,' il dottore si alzò in piedi. 'Era solo un suggerimento, ecco tutto.' 'Come gli è venuta in mente questa pazza idea?' 'Beh, è solo un suggerimento.' 'Ma, Jones, come posso iniziare, cioè, sono abbastanza folle per farlo?' 'Ho pensato anche a questo. Posso trovare uno studente da una delle scuole d'arte. Verrebbe qui una volta alla settimana a darle lezioni. Se dopo un po' di tempo non le piace, potrà mandarlo via.' Il Dottor Jones andò dal suo amico, il direttore di un Istituto d'Arte, e gli spiegò la situazione. Il direttore trovò una persona adatta – un giovane di diciotto anni di nome Frank Swain, uno studente dotato. Aveva bisogno di quel denaro. Lavorava di notte come fattorino per pagarsi gli studi all'Istituto.
pages.english_text: 'All right,' the doctor stood up. 'It was just a suggestion, that's all.' 'Where did you get this crazy idea?' 'Well, it's only a suggestion.' 'But, Jones, how can I start, that is, if I am foolish enough to do it?' 'I've thought of that too. I can get a student from one of the art schools. He would come here once a week and give you lessons. If you don't like it after a little while, you can send him away.' Doctor Jones went to his friend, the director of an Art Institute, and explained the situation. The director found a suitable person - a young man of eighteen named Frank Swain, who was a gifted student. He needed the money. He was working as a bell-boy at night to pay for his studies at the Institute.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Lavorava di notte come fattorino per pagarsi gli studi.'
translation.answer: He was working as a bell-boy at night to pay for his studies.

comprehension.question: Who did the doctor find to give art lessons to Mr. Smith?
comprehension.answer: Frank Swain, an 18-year-old gifted student.

comprehension.question: Why did Frank need the money?
comprehension.answer: To pay for his studies at the Art Institute.

pages.italian_text: Il giovane, venne presentato al dottore. Potete immaginare la sua felicità quando udì l'offerta del dottore. Cinque dollari a lezione! Bene! Il pomeriggio seguente Frank andò allo studio del Signor Smith. Il vecchio lo guardò con sospetto. 'Signore, non sono ancora un artista,' disse il giovane. Il vecchio mormorò qualcosa. Frank preparò della carta e delle matite sopra al tavolo. 'Proviamo a disegnare quel vaso laggiù,' suggerì. 'Umph.' Il vecchio prese una matita e fece uno scarabocchio. Fece un altro scarabocchio e li unì con un paio di linee. Poi guardò il risultato con soddisfazione.
pages.english_text: The young man was introduced to the doctor. You may imagine how delighted he was when he heard the doctor's offer. Five dollars a lesson! Fine! The next afternoon Frank came to Mr. Smith's study. The old man looked at him suspiciously. 'Sir, I am not an artist yet,' said the young man. The old man murmured something. Frank arranged some paper and pencils on the table. 'Let's try to draw that vase over there,' he suggested. 'Umph.' The old man took a pencil and made a scrawl. He made another scrawl and connected them with a couple of lines. Then he looked at the result with satisfaction.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Proviamo a disegnare quel vaso laggiù.'
translation.answer: Let's try to draw that vase over there.

comprehension.question: How much was Frank paid per lesson?
comprehension.answer: Five dollars.

comprehension.question: What did Mr. Smith draw first?
comprehension.answer: A scrawl, then another scrawl connected with lines.

pages.italian_text: Frank era paziente. Aveva bisogno di quei cinque dollari. 'Se vuole disegnare qualcosa deve guardare a cosa sta disegnando, signore. Vuole riguardare il vaso un'altra volta?' disse Frank gentilmente. Il vecchio obbedì. Poi disse: 'Quel vaso è piuttosto carino. Non lo avevo mai notato prima.' In quel momento entrò il servitore portando un bicchiere di succo per il suo padrone. 'Oh, ancora succo d'arancia,' disse il Signor Smith. Frank se ne andò. Quando ritornò la settimana seguente, trovò un disegno sul tavolo che aveva una certa somiglianza con il vaso. Il vecchio gli chiese: 'Beh, che ne pensi di questo?' 'Non male, signore,' rispose Frank. 'Ma è un po' storto.'
pages.english_text: Frank was patient. He needed the five dollars. 'If you want to draw something you'll have to look at what you are drawing, sir. Will you look at the vase again?' Frank said gently. The old man obeyed. Then he said: 'The vase is really quite pretty. I never noticed it before.' At that moment the servant came in bringing a glass of juice for his master. 'Oh, it's orange juice again,' said Mr. Smith. Frank left. When he came the following week there was a drawing on the table that had a slight resemblance to the vase. The old man asked him: 'Well, what do you think of it?' 'Not bad, sir,' answered Frank. 'But it's a little crooked.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Non lo avevo mai notato prima.'
translation.answer: I never noticed it before.

comprehension.question: What did Mr. Smith notice about the vase?
comprehension.answer: That it was quite pretty, something he had never noticed before.

comprehension.question: How did Frank critique the drawing the following week?
comprehension.answer: He said it was not bad but a little crooked.

pages.italian_text: 'Capisco. Le due metà non coincidono,' il vecchio era d'accordo. Aggiunse alcune linee e colorò lo spazio aperto con una matita blu. Poi guardò verso la porta. 'Ascolta, ragazzo,' sussurrò, 'Voglio chiederti una cosa prima che il vecchio 'succo d'arancia' ritorni.' 'Sì, signore,' rispose Frank con rispetto. 'Potresti venire due volte alla settimana o magari tre volte?' 'Certamente, Signor Smith.' 'Bene. Facciamo lunedì, mercoledì e venerdì. Alle ore quattro.' Il servitore entrò nella stanza e fu sorpreso nel vedere che questa volta il suo padrone era disposto a prendere il succo.
pages.english_text: 'I see. The halves do not match,' the old man agreed. He added a few lines and colored the open spaces with a blue pencil. Then he looked towards the door. 'Listen, young man,' he whispered, 'I want to ask you something before old 'orange juice' comes back.' 'Yes, sir,' replied Frank respectfully. 'Could you come twice a week or perhaps three times?' 'Surely, Mr. Smith.' 'Good. Let's make it Monday, Wednesday and Friday. Four o'clock.' The servant entered the room and was surprised to see that this time his master was willing to take his juice.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Le due metà non coincidono.'
translation.answer: The halves do not match.

comprehension.question: How often did Mr. Smith want Frank to come for lessons?
comprehension.answer: Three times a week (Monday, Wednesday, Friday).

comprehension.question: What surprised the servant when he entered?
comprehension.answer: That Mr. Smith was willing to take his juice.

pages.italian_text: Mentre passavano le settimane, le visite di Frank diventavano sempre più frequenti. Ora, quando il dottore veniva a visitare il Signor Smith, il vecchio parlava molto di arte. Mostrava anche con orgoglio le macchie di pittura sulla sua vestaglia di seta pesante. Non permetteva che il suo servitore la portasse in tintoria. Il motivo era che voleva mostrare al dottore quanto si stava impegnando. Il consiglio del dottore sembrava funzionare con il Signor Smith. Non ci furono più acquisti di aziende che costavano un sacco di denaro. Niente più transazioni folli che rovinavano la sua salute. L'arte era una cura completa per i suoi guai finanziari.
pages.english_text: As weeks went by Frank's visits became more frequent. Now when the doctor came to see Mr. Smith, the old man was talking a lot about art. He also proudly demonstrated the stains of paint on his heavy silk dressing gown. He did not allow his servant to send it to the cleaner's. The reason was that he wanted to show the doctor how hard he had been working. The doctor's advice appeared to be working for Mr. Smith. There were no more purchases of companies that cost a lot of money. No more crazy transactions which ruined his health. Art was a complete cure for his financial troubles.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'L'arte era una cura completa per i suoi guai finanziari.'
translation.answer: Art was a complete cure for his financial troubles.

comprehension.question: How did Mr. Smith show the doctor his commitment to art?
comprehension.answer: He proudly showed the paint stains on his dressing gown and refused to send it to the cleaner's.

comprehension.question: What positive change occurred in Mr. Smith's behavior?
comprehension.answer: He stopped making expensive purchases and crazy transactions.

pages.italian_text: Il dottore permise al suo paziente di visitare gallerie d'arte e mostre con Frank. Un mondo completamente nuovo aprì i suoi misteri. Il vecchio sembrava mostrare un certo interesse nelle gallerie e sui pittori esposti in esse. Come erano gestite le gallerie? Chi selezionava i dipinti per le mostre? Un'idea gli si stava formando nel cervello. Quando arrivò la primavera e gli alberi erano in fioritura, il Signor Smith fece un quadro che chiamò 'Alberi Vestiti di Bianco.' Il quadro era orribile ma tuttavia il vecchio annunciò che voleva esibirlo nella mostra estiva alla Lathrop Gallery.
pages.english_text: The doctor allowed his patient to visit art galleries and exhibitions with Frank. An entirely new world opened up its mysteries. The old man seemed to take a great interest in the galleries and the painters displayed in them. How were the galleries managed? Who selected the pictures for the exhibitions? An idea was forming in his brain. When spring came and the trees were in bloom Mr. Smith made a picture which he called 'Trees Dressed in White'. The picture was awful but nevertheless the old man announced that he wanted to exhibit it in the summer show at the Lathrop Gallery.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Un'idea gli si stava formando nel cervello.'
translation.answer: An idea was forming in his brain.

comprehension.question: What did Mr. Smith paint in the spring?
comprehension.answer: A picture called 'Trees Dressed in White'.

comprehension.question: Where did he want to exhibit his painting?
comprehension.answer: At the summer show at the Lathrop Gallery.

pages.italian_text: La mostra estiva in questa galleria era la più grande mostra d'arte dell'anno come qualità, se non anche in grandezza. Il sogno di una vita di qualsiasi artista negli Stati Uniti era un premio Lathrop. Ed era a questa mostra che il Signor Smith voleva esibire 'Alberi Vestiti di Bianco,' che sembrava del condimento per insalata che qualcuno aveva gettato sul muro di una casa. 'Se i giornali scrivono un articolo su questo, il Signor Smith diventerà uno zimbello. Dobbiamo fermarlo,' Frank disse con orrore. 'No,' protestò il dottore. 'Non possiamo farlo ora. Rovineremo tutto il buon lavoro fatto.'
pages.english_text: The summer show at this gallery was the biggest art exhibition of the year in quality, if not in size. The lifetime dream of every artist in the United States was a Lathrop prize. And it was in this show that Mr. Smith was going to exhibit his 'Trees Dressed in White,' which looked like salad dressing which somebody had thrown on the wall of a house. 'If the papers write about this, Mr. Smith will become a laughing stock. We must stop him,' Frank said in horror. 'No,' protested the doctor. 'We can't do that now. We may spoil all the good work we have done.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Il Signor Smith diventerà uno zimbello.'
translation.answer: Mr. Smith will become a laughing stock.

comprehension.question: What did Frank want to do about the painting?
comprehension.answer: Stop Mr. Smith from exhibiting it.

comprehension.question: Why did the doctor disagree?
comprehension.answer: Because stopping him might spoil all the good work they had done.

pages.italian_text: Con sorpresa di tutti e tre - e soprattutto di Frank - 'Alberi Vestiti di Bianco' fu accettato per la Mostra Lathrop. Non era soltanto il Signor Smith pazzo, pensò Frank, ma anche la Lathrop Gallery era pazza. Fortunatamente, il dipinto venne esposto in un angolo buio dove i visitatori riuscivano a malapena a vederlo. Un pomeriggio Frank arrivò alla galleria e arrossì fino alle orecchie quando vide 'Alberi Vestiti di Bianco' - un orribile spruzzo sul muro. Quando due studenti si fermarono davanti al quadro, ridendo, Frank scappò via terrorizzato. Non voleva sentire cosa questi due giovani avevano da dire.
pages.english_text: To the astonishment of all three - and especially Frank - 'Trees Dressed in White' was accepted for the Lathrop Show. Not only was Mr. Smith crazy, thought Frank, but the Lathrop Gallery was crazy too. Fortunately, the painting was displayed in a dark corner where visitors could hardly see it. Frank came to the gallery one afternoon and blushed to the ears when he saw 'Trees Dressed in White' - an ugly splash on the wall. When two students stopped before the picture, laughing, Frank ran away in terror. He did not want to hear what these young men had to say.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Frank scappò via terrorizzato.'
translation.answer: Frank ran away in terror.

comprehension.question: Where was the painting displayed in the gallery?
comprehension.answer: In a dark corner where visitors could hardly see it.

comprehension.question: How did Frank react when students laughed at the painting?
comprehension.answer: He ran away in terror.

pages.italian_text: Durante il corso della mostra il vecchio continuò a prendere lezioni e non parlò del suo quadro. Ma ogni volta che Frank entrava nella stanza trovava il Signor Smith che ridacchiava. Forse il vecchio era veramente pazzo. Ed era strano che la commissione di Lathrop lo avesse incoraggiato accettando il suo dipinto. Due giorni prima della chiusura della mostra, una lunga busta dall'aspetto ufficiale fu recapitata al Signor Smith mentre Frank, Koppel e il dottore si trovavano nella stanza. 'Leggimela,' chiese il vecchio. 'I miei occhi sono stanchi dal dipingere.' 'È con piacere che la Lathrop Gallery annuncia che il Primo Premio di 1,000 dollari è stato assegnato al Signor Collis P. Smith per il suo dipinto 'Alberi Vestiti di Bianco'.'
pages.english_text: During the course of the show the old man continued taking his lessons and did not talk about his picture. But every time Frank entered the room he found Mr. Smith chuckling. May be the old man was really crazy. And it was strange that the Lathrop committee encouraged him by accepting his picture. Two days before the close of the exhibition a long official looking envelope was delivered to Mr. Smith while Frank, Koppel and the doctor were in the room. 'Read it to me,' asked the old man. 'My eyes are tired from painting.' 'It gives the Lathrop Gallery pleasure to announce that the First Prize of $1,000 has been awarded to Mr. Collis P. Smith for his painting 'Trees Dressed in White'.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'I miei occhi sono stanchi dal dipingere.'
translation.answer: My eyes are tired from painting.

comprehension.question: What surprising news did the letter contain?
comprehension.answer: That Mr. Smith had won the first prize of $1,000.

comprehension.question: Who read the letter to Mr. Smith?
comprehension.answer: Frank, Koppel, or the doctor – the old man asked someone to read it, but the text doesn't specify who; likely Frank or the doctor.

pages.italian_text: Frank e Koppel rimasero stupiti. Il dottore mostrando il suo solito autocontrollo professionale disse: 'Congratulazioni, Signor Smith! Certo, non mi aspettavo delle notizie così belle. Ma, ma - ora dovrà ammettere che l'arte dà molte più soddisfazioni rispetto agli affari.' 'L'arte non è niente,' disse il vecchio. 'Ho acquistato la Lathrop Gallery lo scorso mese.'
pages.english_text: Frank and Koppel were astonished. The doctor showing his usual professional self-control said: 'Congratulations, Mr. Smith! Of course, I did not expect such great news. But, but - now you'll have to admit that art brings much more satisfaction than business.' 'Art is nothing,' said the old man. 'I bought the Lathrop Gallery last month.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Ho acquistato la Lathrop Gallery lo scorso mese.'
translation.answer: I bought the Lathrop Gallery last month.

comprehension.question: What was the doctor's reaction to the prize?
comprehension.answer: He congratulated Mr. Smith and said art brings more satisfaction than business.

comprehension.question: What was the real reason Mr. Smith's painting won first prize?
comprehension.answer: He had bought the Lathrop Gallery the previous month.

overall_questions.question: What was Mr. Smith's main problem at the beginning of the story?
overall_questions.answer: He had heart attacks from buying businesses that went bankrupt, and he refused to follow the doctor's orders.

overall_questions.question: What did the doctor suggest to keep Mr. Smith from making bad purchases?
overall_questions.answer: Studying art just for fun.

overall_questions.question: Who was Frank Swain?
overall_questions.answer: An 18-year-old gifted art student who worked as a bellboy to pay for his studies.

overall_questions.question: How much was Frank paid per lesson?
overall_questions.answer: Five dollars.

overall_questions.question: How did Mr. Smith's attitude toward art change over time?
overall_questions.answer: He became very interested, talked a lot about art, and started painting himself.

overall_questions.question: What painting did Mr. Smith create and want to exhibit?
overall_questions.answer: 'Trees Dressed in White'.

overall_questions.question: Why was Frank horrified about the painting being exhibited?
overall_questions.answer: Because it was awful and Mr. Smith would become a laughing stock.

overall_questions.question: What surprising announcement was made two days before the exhibition closed?
overall_questions.answer: Mr. Smith won the first prize of $1,000.

overall_questions.question: How did Mr. Smith explain winning the prize?
overall_questions.answer: He said 'Art is nothing. I bought the Lathrop Gallery last month.'

overall_questions.question: What is the irony at the end of the story?
overall_questions.answer: Mr. Smith didn't improve his business habits; he simply bought the gallery to win his own prize.

pages.italian_text: La vidi allo spettacolo teatrale e durante la pausa mi avvicinai e mi sedetti di fianco a lei. Era passato tanto tempo da quando ci incontrammo ma mi riconobbe subito e mi salutò con voce amichevole. 'Ti ricordi della prima volta che ti ho visto? Mi volevi portare a pranzo.' Mi ricordavo? Accadde venti anni fa ed io vivevo a Parigi. Avevo un piccolo appartamento nel quartiere Latino e guadagnavo quel tanto che bastava per tenere il mio corpo e la mia anima d'un pezzo. Aveva letto un mio libro e mi aveva scritto a riguardo. Risposi, ringraziandola, e poi ricevetti un'altra sua lettera in cui mi informava che sarebbe passata da Parigi e avrebbe apprezzato fare due chiacchiere con me.
pages.english_text: I saw her at the theater play and during the break I came over and sat down beside her. It was long since we have met but she recognized me at once and addressed me in a friendly voice. 'Do you remember the first time I saw you? You asked me to a luncheon.' Did I remember? It was twenty years ago and I was living in Paris. I had a small apartment in the Latin Quarter and I was earning only just enough to keep my body and soul together. She had read a book of mine and had written to me about it. I answered, thanking her, and then I received from her another letter saying that she was passing through Paris and would like to have a chat with me.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Guadagnavo quel tanto che bastava per tenere il mio corpo e la mia anima d'un pezzo.'
translation.answer: I was earning only just enough to keep my body and soul together.

comprehension.question: How long ago did the luncheon incident take place?
comprehension.answer: Twenty years ago.

comprehension.question: Where did the narrator live at that time?
comprehension.answer: In a small apartment in the Latin Quarter of Paris.

pages.italian_text: Il giovedì seguente, disse, avrebbe speso la mattinata in Lussemburgo e mi chiese se l'avrei portata a pranzo da Foyet più tardi. Foyet's è un ristorante in cui mangiano i senatori francesi ed era così fuori dalla mia portata che non ci avevo mai pensato. Ma ero lusingato e troppo giovane per dire di no a una signora. Le dissi che avrei fatto volentieri colazione con lei. 'Alle dodici?' 'Non è troppo tardi?' 'Colazione? Preferirei di no. Di solito non mangio niente prima delle dodici, ma va bene così.' 'Mi unirò a te allora.' 'Non mangio mai più di una sola cosa. Penso che la gente mangi troppo al giorno d'oggi. Un po' di pesce, forse. Mi domando se abbiano del salmone.'
pages.english_text: On the following Thursday, she said, she would spend the morning at the Luxembourg and asked me whether I would take her to a little luncheon at Foyet's afterwards. Foyet's is a restaurant at which the French senators eat and it was so far beyond my means that I had never even thought of it. But I was flattered and I was too young to say no to a lady. I told her that I would be delighted to have breakfast with her. 'At twelve?' 'Isn't it too late?' 'Breakfast? I would prefer not. I never eat anything before twelve, but that will be fine.' 'I'll join you then.' 'I never eat more than one thing. I think people eat too much nowadays. A little fish, perhaps. I wonder if they have any salmon.'

vocabulary.question: What does 'portata' mean in 'fuori dalla mia portata'?

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Ero troppo giovane per dire di no a una signora.'
translation.answer: I was too young to say no to a lady.

comprehension.question: Why was Foyet's restaurant beyond the narrator's means?
comprehension.answer: It was a restaurant where French senators ate, implying it was very expensive.

comprehension.question: What did the woman say she never eats more than?
comprehension.answer: One thing.

pages.italian_text: Beh, era piuttosto presto durante l'anno per il salmone e non era sul menù, ma chiesi al cameriere se ce ne fosse. Sì, un bellissimo salmone era appena stato consegnato, disse il cameriere, lo ordinai per la mia ospite. Il cameriere le chiese se desiderasse qualcosa nell'attesa che il salmone venisse cucinato. 'No,' rispose, 'Non mangio mai più di una cosa, a meno che non abbiate un po' di caviale. Apprezzo sempre il caviale.' Il mio cuore perse un battito. Sapevo di non potermi permettere del caviale, ma non potevo dirglielo. Dissi al cameriere di portare del caviale. Per me ordinai il piatto meno costoso sul menù, cioè una costata di montone.
pages.english_text: Well, it was early in the year for salmon and it was not on the menu, but I asked the waiter if there was any. Yes, a beautiful salmon had just been delivered, the waiter said, I ordered it for my guest. The waiter asked her if she would have something while salmon was being cooked. 'No,' she answered, 'I never eat more than one thing, unless you have a little caviar. I never mind caviar.' My heart skipped a beat. I knew I could not afford caviar, but I could not tell her that. I told the waiter to bring caviar. For myself I ordered the cheapest dish on the menu and that was a mutton chop.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Il mio cuore perse un battito.'
translation.answer: My heart skipped a beat.

comprehension.question: What did the narrator order for himself?
comprehension.answer: The cheapest dish on the menu, a mutton chop.

comprehension.question: Why did the narrator's heart skip a beat?
comprehension.answer: Because he knew he could not afford caviar but felt he could not say no.

pages.italian_text: 'Non ti consiglio di mangiare carne,' disse lei. 'Non so come tu possa lavorare dopo aver mangiato qualcosa di così pesante come le costate.' Poi il cameriere si avvicinò a noi e chiese se ci piacerebbe bere. 'Non bevo mai nulla per pranzo,' disse lei. 'Nemmeno io,' risposi velocemente. 'Tranne il vino bianco' continuò lei come se io non avessi parlato. 'Questi vini bianchi Francesi sono così leggeri. Sono fantastici per la digestione.' 'Cosa gradiresti?' chiesi educatamente. 'Il mio dottore non mi fa bere nulla fuorché champagne.' Ordinai mezza bottiglia e dissi che il mio dottore mi aveva assolutamente proibito di bere champagne. 'Cosa berrai allora?' 'Acqua.'
pages.english_text: 'I don't advise you to eat meat,' she said. 'I don't know how you can work after eating heavy things like chops.' Then the waiter came up to us and asked if we'd like to drink. 'I never drink anything for luncheon,' she said. 'Neither do I,' I answered quickly. 'Except white wine' she continued as if I had not spoken. 'These French white wines are so light. They are wonderful for the digestion.' 'What would you like?' I asked her politely. 'My doctor will not let me drink anything but champagne.' I ordered half a bottle and said that my doctor had absolutely forbidden me to drink champagne. 'What are you going to drink then?' 'Water.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Il mio dottore non mi fa bere nulla fuorché champagne.'
translation.answer: My doctor will not let me drink anything but champagne.

comprehension.question: What drink did the woman claim her doctor allowed?
comprehension.answer: Champagne.

comprehension.question: What did the narrator say he would drink?
comprehension.answer: Water.

pages.italian_text: Mangiò il caviale e poi il salmone. Parlò allegramente di arte e letteratura e di musica. Ma io mi domandavo quanto dovrò pagare per quel pranzo. Quando la mia costata di montone arrivò lei iniziò a rimproverarmi. 'Vedo che hai l'abitudine di pranzare pesante. Sono sicura sia un errore. Perché non segui il mio esempio e mangi una sola cosa? Sono sicura che ti sentiresti molto meglio per questo.' 'Io sto mangiando una sola cosa,' dissi io mentre il cameriere tornava col menù. 'No, no,' lei disse. 'Non mangio mai nient'altro per pranzo. Solo una cosa! Non voglio mai più di questo, ma anche questo io mangio più come una scusa per la conversazione che ogni altra cosa. Non riuscirei proprio a mangiare ancora – a meno che non abbiano un po' di quegli asparagi giganti. Mi dispiacerebbe lasciare Parigi senza mangiarne un po'.'
pages.english_text: She ate the caviar and then the salmon. She talked gaily of art and literature and music. But I wondered how much I will have to pay for that luncheon. When my mutton chop arrived she began to scold me. 'I see that you have a habit of eating a heavy luncheon. I am sure it's a mistake. Why don't you follow my example and just eat one thing? I am sure you would feel much better for it.' 'I am eating only one thing,' I said as the waiter came again with the menu. 'No, no,' she said to him. 'I never eat anything else for luncheon. Only one thing! I never want more than that, and even that I eat more as an excuse for conversation than anything else. I couldn't possibly eat anything more - unless they have some of those giant asparagus. I should be sorry to leave Paris without eating some of them.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Mi dispiacerebbe lasciare Parigi senza mangiarne un po'.'
translation.answer: I should be sorry to leave Paris without eating some of them.

comprehension.question: What did the woman order after the salmon?
comprehension.answer: Giant asparagus.

comprehension.question: How did the narrator feel while she was ordering more food?
comprehension.answer: He was worried about the cost.

pages.italian_text: Il mio cuore perse un battito. Sapevo che erano estremamente costosi. Ma dovevo anche ordinare gli asparagi. Il panico mi assalì. Non era più una questione di quanti soldi avrei avuto per il resto del mese, ma se ne ho abbastanza per pagare il conto. Sarebbe orribile non avere abbastanza soldi per pagare il conto ed essere costretto a farmi prestare qualcosa dalla mia ospite. Sapevo esattamente quanto avevo e se il conto fosse ammontato a più, decisi che avrei messo la mano in tasca e con un grido drammatico avrei annunciato che il mio portafogli era stato rubato. Ovviamente sarebbe imbarazzante se anche lei non avesse avuto abbastanza soldi per pagare il conto. In tal caso avrei potuto solo lasciare il mio orologio e dire che sarei tornato più tardi a pagare il conto.
pages.english_text: My heart skipped a beat. I knew that they were extremely expensive. But I had to order asparagus too. Panic seized me. It was no more a question of how much money would I have left for the rest of the month, but whether I have enough to pay the bill. It would be horrible not to have enough money to pay the bill and be compelled to borrow from my guest. I knew exactly how much I had and if the bill would amount to more, I decided that I would put my hand in my pocket and with a dramatic cry will say that my wallet has been stolen. Obviously it would be embarrassing if she also did not have enough money to pay the bill. In that case, I could just leave my watch and say I would come back later to pay the bill.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Il panico mi assalì.'
translation.answer: Panic seized me.

comprehension.question: What plan did the narrator devise if he couldn't pay the bill?
comprehension.answer: He would pretend his wallet was stolen, or leave his watch and come back later.

comprehension.question: Why was he worried about borrowing from his guest?
comprehension.answer: It would be embarrassing.

pages.italian_text: Gli asparagi comparvero. Erano enormi, succulenti e appetitosi. Mentre la mia compagna mangiava gli asparagi io parlai della condizione nei Balcani. Finalmente lei finì. 'Caffè?' Chiesi. 'Sì, solo un gelato e un caffè,' rispose. Così, ordinai del caffè per me e del gelato e del caffè per lei. 'Sai, c'è una cosa in cui credo,' disse mentre gustava il gelato. 'Bisognerebbe sempre alzarsi da tavola con la sensazione di poter ancora mangiare un po' di più.' 'Hai ancora fame?' Chiesi con orrore. 'Oh, no, non ho fame, vedi, di solito a pranzo non mangio. Ho una tazza di caffè al mattino e poi cena, ma non mangio mai più di una cosa a pranzo.' 'Ah, capisco!'
pages.english_text: The asparagus appeared. They were enormous, succulent and appetizing. While my companion ate the asparagus I spoke of the condition in the Balkans. At last she finished. 'Coffee?' I asked. 'Yes, just an ice cream and coffee,' she answered. So, I ordered coffee for myself and ice cream and coffee for her. 'You know, there is one thing I believe in,' she said as she savored the ice cream. 'One should always get up from a meal feeling that he could eat a little more.' 'Are you still hungry?' I asked with horror. 'Oh, no, I am not hungry, you see, usually I don't eat luncheon. I have a cup of coffee in the morning and then dinner, but I never eat more than one thing for luncheon.' 'Oh, I see!'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Bisognerebbe sempre alzarsi da tavola con la sensazione di poter ancora mangiare un po' di più.'
translation.answer: One should always get up from a meal feeling that he could eat a little more.

comprehension.question: What did the woman order for dessert?
comprehension.answer: Ice cream and coffee.

comprehension.question: What did she say about her usual eating habits?
comprehension.answer: She usually doesn't eat luncheon, only a cup of coffee in the morning and then dinner.

pages.italian_text: Poi, una cosa terribile accadde. Mentre attendevamo il caffè, il capo cameriere si avvicinò a noi con un cesto pieno di pesche. Di sicuro le pesche non erano di stagione allora. Dio solo sa quanto costavano. Anche io lo seppi - poco più tardi, perché la mia ospite, mentre continuava la sua conversazione, ne prese distrattamente una. 'Sai, tu ti sei riempito lo stomaco di carne - e non puoi più mangiare. Io ho appena fatto uno spuntino e posso godermi una pesca.' Il conto arrivò e quando lo pagai, notai che potevo dare solo una piccola mancia. Lei guardò i tre franchi che lasciai al cameriere e seppi che lei mi considerò cattivo. Ma quando uscii dal ristorante avevo tutto un mese di fronte a me e nemmeno un penny in tasca.
pages.english_text: Then a terrible thing happened. While we were waiting for the coffee, the head waiter came up to us with a basket full of peaches. But surely peaches were not in season then. Lord knew what they cost. I knew too - a little later, because my guest, going on with her conversation, absentmindedly took one. 'You know, you have filled your stomach with a lot of meat - and you can't eat any more. I've just had a snack and I can enjoy a peach.' The bill came and when I paid it, I found that I had only enough for a very small tip. She looked at the three francs that I left for the waiter and I knew that she thought I was mean. But when I walked out of the restaurant I had the whole month before me and not a penny in my pocket.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Avevo tutto un mese di fronte a me e nemmeno un penny in tasca.'
translation.answer: I had the whole month before me and not a penny in my pocket.

comprehension.question: What fruit did the woman take from the basket?
comprehension.answer: A peach.

comprehension.question: How much tip did the narrator leave?
comprehension.answer: Three francs.

pages.italian_text: 'Segui il mio esempio,' disse mentre ci stringevamo la mano, 'e non mangi mai più di una sola cosa per pranzo.' 'Farò di meglio,' risposi. 'Non mangerò nulla per cena stasera.' 'Sei spiritoso!' esclamò allegramente, saltando in un taxi. 'Sei davvero spiritoso!' Non sono un uomo cattivo, ma guardarla ora mi faceva pensare di aver finalmente ottenuto la mia vendetta. Oggi, lei pesa più di 130 chili.
pages.english_text: 'Follow my example,' she said as we shook hands, 'and never eat more than one thing for luncheon.' 'I'll do better than that,' I answered. 'I'll eat nothing for dinner tonight.' 'You're a humorist!' she cried gaily, jumping into a cab. 'You are quite a humorist!' I am not a bad man, but looking at her now I thought that I had my revenge at last. Today she weighs over 130 kilograms.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Non mangerò nulla per cena stasera.'
translation.answer: I'll eat nothing for dinner tonight.

comprehension.question: What revenge did the narrator feel he had obtained?
comprehension.answer: The woman now weighs over 130 kilograms.

comprehension.question: What did the narrator say he would do for dinner?
comprehension.answer: Eat nothing.

overall_questions.question: Where did the narrator first meet the woman for luncheon?
overall_questions.answer: At Foyet's restaurant in Paris.

overall_questions.question: What was the narrator's financial situation at the time?
overall_questions.answer: He was poor, earning just enough to keep body and soul together.

overall_questions.question: What did the woman claim she never ate more than?
overall_questions.answer: One thing for luncheon.

overall_questions.question: What did she order to eat?
overall_questions.answer: Salmon, then caviar, then giant asparagus, then ice cream, coffee, and a peach.

overall_questions.question: What did the narrator order for himself?
overall_questions.answer: A mutton chop (the cheapest dish) and water.

overall_questions.question: Why did the narrator panic during the meal?
overall_questions.answer: Because he realized he might not have enough money to pay the bill.

overall_questions.question: What did the narrator plan to do if he couldn't pay?
overall_questions.answer: Pretend his wallet was stolen or leave his watch.

overall_questions.question: How much tip did he leave?
overall_questions.answer: Three francs.

overall_questions.question: What did the woman think of the small tip?
overall_questions.answer: She thought he was mean (cattivo).

overall_questions.question: What ironic detail does the narrator reveal at the end?
overall_questions.answer: The woman now weighs over 130 kilograms.

pages.italian_text: Il fatto avvenne a Laredo in una casa da gioco. I giocatori erano seduti davanti a una partita di poker quando iniziò un litigio riguardo ad alcune carte. Un giovanotto, dell'età di Kid più o meno, prese il suo revolver, e sfiorò appena l'orecchio destro di Kid. Quando arrivò il turno di Kid, sparò al giovane e scappò via. Gli amici del giovane superarono Kid alla stazione. Kid si girò e puntò il suo revolver verso di loro. Era un buon tiratore. Vedendo il suo revolver si fermarono, si girarono e sparirono. Lo stesso pomeriggio, Kid prese un treno, ma alla prossima stazione scese perché c'erano dei pali del telegrafo lungo la linea e Kid non si fidava dell'elettricità, si sentiva più sicuro a cavallo. Sapeva che l'uomo a cui aveva sparato proveniva da un ranch dove le persone erano vendicative.
pages.english_text: It happened in Laredo in a gambling house. The players were sitting at a poker game when a quarrel began over some cards. A young fellow of about Kid's age pulled out his revolver, but just grazed Kid's right ear. When Kid's turn came, he shot the young man and ran away. The young man's friends overtook Kid at the station. Kid turned and aimed his revolver at them. He was a good shot. Seeing his revolver they stopped, turned and disappeared. The same afternoon Kid took a train, but at the next station he left it because there were telegraph posts along the line and Kid did not trust electricity, he felt safer on horseback. He knew that the man whom he shot came from a ranch where the people were vengeful.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Era un buon tiratore.'
translation.answer: He was a good shot.

comprehension.question: Where did the initial quarrel take place?
comprehension.answer: In a gambling house in Laredo.

comprehension.question: Why did Kid leave the train at the next station?
comprehension.answer: Because he didn't trust electricity (telegraph poles) and felt safer on horseback.

pages.italian_text: C'era un negozio vicino alla stazione e Kid vide un cavallo sellato tra gli alberi. Lo prese e partì al galoppo. Dopo tre giorni si trovò in riva al mare. Lo stesso giorno s'imbarcò su una nave a vapore con un carico di legname e cerini. Thacker, il console degli Stati Uniti a Buenas Tierras, non era ancora ubriaco. Erano solo le undici del mattino e non era mai ubriaco fino a metà pomeriggio. Quindi, quando vide Kid in piedi davanti alla porta del consolato, era ancora in condizione di mostrare l'ospitalità di un rappresentante di una grande nazione. 'Mi scusi,' disse Kid. 'Mi è stato detto che di solito le persone vengono da voi prima di andare a vedere il paese. Sono appena arrivato dal Texas.' 'Piacere di conoscerla Signor - ?' disse il console. 'Dalton,' disse Kid. 'Ma mi chiamano semplicemente Kid nel paese di Rio Grande.'
pages.english_text: There was a store near the station and Kid saw a saddled horse among the trees. He took it and galloped on. After three days he was on the sea shore. The same day he sailed on a steamer with a cargo of timber and matches. Thacker, the United States consul at Buenas Tierras, was not yet drunk. It was only eleven o'clock in the morning and he was never drunk until the middle of the afternoon. So, when he saw Kid standing at the door of consulate, he was still in a condition to show the hospitality of a representative of a great nation. 'I am sorry,' said Kid. 'I was told that people usually called on you before going to see the town. I have just come from Texas.' 'Nice to meet you Mr.- ?' said the consul. 'Dalton,' said Kid. 'But they simply call me Kid in the Rio Grande country.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Non era mai ubriaco fino a metà pomeriggio.'
translation.answer: He was never drunk until the middle of the afternoon.

comprehension.question: What did Kid do after taking the horse?
comprehension.answer: He galloped for three days until he reached the sea shore, then sailed on a steamer.

comprehension.question: Who was Thacker?
comprehension.answer: The United States consul at Buenas Tierras.

pages.italian_text: 'Io mi chiamo Thacker,' disse il console. 'Prenda una sedia. Se ha intenzione di acquistare del terreno, sarò molto lieto di aiutarla. Inoltre, parlano lo spagnolo qui e le servirà un interprete.' 'Non ho intenzione di acquistare nulla,' disse Kid, 'e parlo lo spagnolo molto meglio dell'inglese.' 'Lei parla lo spagnolo?' disse Thacker pensieroso. Guardò Kid in silenzio. 'Sembri anche uno spagnolo,' continuò, 'e non puoi avere più di venti o ventuno anni. Sei coraggioso?' 'Cosa intende dire?' chiese Kid, alzandosi improvvisamente e avvicinandosi al console. 'Sei pronto ad accettare qualsiasi lavoro?' chiese Thacker. 'Le devo dire,' disse Kid, 'che ho preso parte in un piccolo litigio a Laredo e ho ucciso un uomo. Siccome avevo paura che i suoi amici volessero vendicare la sua morte, sono stato costretto a lasciare il paese e venire qui. Quindi, come può vedere sono pronto ad accettare qualsiasi lavoro.'
pages.english_text: 'My name's Thacker,' said the consul. 'Take a chair. If you intend to buy land, I'll be very glad to help you. Besides, they speak Spanish here and you will need an interpreter.' 'I am not buying anything,' said Kid, 'and I speak Spanish much better than English.' 'You speak Spanish?' said Thacker thoughtfully. He looked at Kid in silence. 'You look like a Spaniard, too,' he continued, 'and you can't be more than twenty or twenty one. Are you brave?' 'What do you mean?' asked Kid, suddenly rising and coming up to the consul. 'Are you ready to accept any job?' asked Thacker. 'I must tell you,' said Kid, 'that I took part in a little quarrel in Laredo and killed a man. As I was afraid that his friends would avenge his death, I was compelled to leave the place and come here. So you see that I am ready to take any work.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Parlo lo spagnolo molto meglio dell'inglese.'
translation.answer: I speak Spanish much better than English.

comprehension.question: What did Kid confess to Thacker?
comprehension.answer: That he killed a man in Laredo and fled because he feared revenge.

comprehension.question: Why did Thacker think Kid might be useful?
comprehension.answer: Because Kid was young, brave, and spoke Spanish.

pages.italian_text: Thacker si alzò e chiuse la porta. 'Mostrami la tua mano,' disse. Prese la mano sinistra di Kid e esaminò il dorso della mano attentamente. 'Lo posso fare,' disse, 'la tua mano guarirà in una settimana.' 'Cosa intende dire?' chiese Kid. Attraverso la finestra, Thacker indicò una casa bianca di ricchi che si trovava in mezzo agli alberi tropicali. 'In quella casa,' disse Thacker, 'un vecchio signore e sua moglie ti stanno aspettando; riempiranno le tue tasche di soldi. Il vecchio Santos Urique vive là. Possiede metà delle miniere d'oro di questo paese.' 'È ubriaco?' chiese Kid. 'Siediti e ti racconterò tutto.' 'Dodici anni fa il vecchio signore e sua moglie persero il loro unico figlio. Aveva otto anni. Alcuni americani, che cercavano l'oro e che visitavano spesso gli Uriques, raccontarono al bambino un sacco di cose meravigliose sugli Stati Uniti; e un mese più tardi il bambino sparì.'
pages.english_text: Thacker got up and closed the door. 'Show me your hand,' he said. He took Kid's left hand and examined the back of the hand very carefully. 'I can do it,' he said, 'your hand will heal in a week.' 'What do you mean?' asked Kid. Through the window, Thacker pointed to a rich white house standing among tropical trees. 'In that house,' said Thacker, 'an old gentleman and his wife are waiting for you; they will fill your pockets with money. Old Santos Urique lives there. He owns half the gold mines in the country.' 'Are you drunk?' asked Kid. 'Sit down and I'll tell you everything. Twelve years ago the old gentleman and his wife lost their only son. He was eight years old. Some Americans, who were looking for gold and who often visited the Uriques, told the boy a lot of wonderful things about the States; and a month later the boy disappeared.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Possiede metà delle miniere d'oro di questo paese.'
translation.answer: He owns half the gold mines in the country.

comprehension.question: What did Thacker say he could do to Kid's hand?
comprehension.answer: Tattoo an eagle on the back of it, which would heal in a week.

comprehension.question: What happened to the Uriques' son?
comprehension.answer: He disappeared after hearing wonderful stories about the United States.

pages.italian_text: 'Venne detto che era stato visto una volta nel Texas. Gli Uriques lo cercarono ovunque, spesero molto denaro, ma invano. La madre era molto ammalata. Dicono che lei creda che suo figlio ritornerà e non hanno mai perso la speranza. Sul dorso della mano sinistra del bambino era tatuata un'aquila in volo.' Kid non rispose nulla. 'Posso farlo,' continuò il console, 'e in una settimana avrai l'aquila tatuata sulla mano. Chiamerò il vecchio Urique e quando vedrà che hai il segno del tatuaggio, che parli lo spagnolo e che sai raccontargli del Texas, i genitori saranno felici. Il resto è molto semplice. Il vecchio Urique tiene in casa circa 100,000 dollari in una cassaforte che anche un bambino saprebbe aprire. Prendi i soldi, li divideremo, prendi una nave a vapore diretta verso Rio Janeiro e lascia che il consolato vada a pezzi. Che ne pensi?' 'Mi piace il suo piano,' disse Kid. In pochi giorni, il tatuaggio sulla mano sinistra di Kid era pronto e la mano era guarita.
pages.english_text: 'It was said that he was seen once in Texas. The Uriques looked for him everywhere, they spent a lot of money, but in vain. The mother was quite ill. They say she believes that her son will come back and they never given up hope. On the back of the boy's left hand was tattooed a flying eagle.' Kid answered nothing. 'I can do it,' continued the consul, 'and in a week you will have the eagle tattooed on your hand. I'll call old Urique and when he sees that you have the tattoo mark, that you speak Spanish and can tell him about Texas, the parents will be happy. The rest of it is very simple. Old Urique keeps in his house about 100,000 dollars in a safe which a child can open. Get the money, we'll divide it, take a steamer going to Rio Janeiro and let the consulate go to pieces. What do you think of it?' 'I like your plan,' said Kid. In a few days the tattoo on Kid's left hand was ready and the hand healed.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Non hanno mai perso la speranza.'
translation.answer: They have never given up hope.

comprehension.question: What was the distinctive mark on the lost son's hand?
comprehension.answer: A tattoo of a flying eagle on the back of his left hand.

comprehension.question: What was Thacker's plan for the money in Urique's safe?
comprehension.answer: Kid would take the money, they would divide it, and escape to Rio Janeiro.

pages.italian_text: Poi il console scrisse la seguente nota al vecchio Urique: 'Egregio Signor Urique, ho il piacere di informarla che ho un ospite in casa mia arrivato dagli Stati Uniti alcuni giorni fa. Penso che lui è vostro figlio e che avesse intenzione di ritornare da voi, ma all'ultimo momento il suo coraggio lo ha abbandonato perché non sa come lo riceverete. Cordiali saluti, Thomson Thacker.' Dopo trenta minuti una carrozza arrivò fino alla porta del consolato. Un uomo alto con i capelli bianchi e una signora vestita di nero uscirono dalla carrozza. Thacker li salutò con l'inchino. Videro un giovane con il volto bruciato dal sole in piedi vicino alla scrivania. Donna Urique guardò il suo volto e la sua mano sinistra, e piangendo disse: 'Oh, mio figlio!' avvolse Kid tra le sue braccia.
pages.english_text: Then the consul wrote the following note to old Urique: 'Dear Mr. Urique, I have the pleasure to inform you that I have a guest in my house who arrived from the United States a few days ago. I think that he is your son and that he intended to return to you, but at the last moment his courage left him because he doesn't know how you will receive him. Yours faithfully, Thomson Thacker.' In thirty minutes a carriage drove up to the door of the consulate. A tall man with white hair and a lady dressed in black got out of it. Thacker met them with the bow. They saw a young man with a sunburnt face standing near a desk. Donna Urique glanced at his face and his left hand, and crying: 'Oh, my son!' she wrapped Kid in her arms.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Donna Urique avvolse Kid tra le sue braccia.'
translation.answer: Donna Urique wrapped Kid in her arms.

comprehension.question: How did the Uriques react when they saw Kid?
comprehension.answer: Donna Urique embraced him, calling him her son.

comprehension.question: What did Thacker's letter claim about the young man?
comprehension.answer: That he was their son who had lost courage to return.

pages.italian_text: Un mese più tardi, Kid venne al consolato dopo aver ricevuto un messaggio da Thacker. Era molto ben vestito e aveva un grosso diamante che brillava sul suo dito. 'Quali notizie ci sono?' chiese Thacker. 'Niente di nuovo.' Rispose Kid. 'È ora di iniziare gli affari. Perché non prendi i soldi? Tutti sanno che la cassaforte di Urique è piena di soldi.' 'Oh, ci sono molti soldi in casa,' disse Kid, guardando il suo diamante. 'E il mio padre adottivo mi ha mostrato dove si trova la chiave della cassaforte.' 'Bene, allora, cosa stai aspettando?' chiese Thacker con rabbia. 'Non dimenticarti che posso dire a Don Urique e a tutti chi sei realmente.' 'Ascolta,' disse Kid. 'Quando mi parli, rivolgiti a me come Don Francisco Urique. Per quanto riguarda il denaro di mio padre, lascialo a lui. Non ho intenzione di prenderlo.' 'Non hai intenzione di darmi la metà allora?' 'Certamente no,' rispose Kid, 'e ti dirò il perché.'
pages.english_text: A month later Kid came to the consulate in answer to a message from Thacker. He was very well dressed and a big diamond shone on his finger. 'What is the news?' asked Thacker. 'Nothing new,' answered Kid. 'It is time to start business. Why don't you get the money? Everybody knows that Urique's safe is full of money.' 'Oh, there is a lot of money in the house,' said Kid, looking at his diamond. 'And my adopted father has shown me where the key of the safe is.' 'Well, then, what are you waiting for?' asked Thacker angrily. 'Don't forget that I can tell Don Urique and everybody who you really are.' 'Listen,' said Kid. 'When you speak to me, address me as Don Francisco Urique. As to my father's money, let him have it. I don't intend to take it.' 'Don't you intend to give me my half then?' 'Of course not,' answered Kid, 'and I'll tell you why.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Rivolgiti a me come Don Francisco Urique.'
translation.answer: Address me as Don Francisco Urique.

comprehension.question: How had Kid's circumstances changed after a month?
comprehension.answer: He was very well dressed and wore a big diamond.

comprehension.question: Why did Kid refuse to take the money from the safe?
comprehension.answer: He had decided to stay as Don Francisco Urique and not betray his adopted parents.

pages.italian_text: 'La prima sera che mi trovai a casa di Don Urique, subito dopo essere andato a letto, la mia nuova madre è entrata e mi ha rimboccato le coperte. 'Mio caro ragazzo', disse, con le lacrime che le scendevano dagli occhi cadendo sul mio viso. Ho avuto poco a che fare con le madri nella mia vita, ma penso che è meglio continuare ad ingannare questa madre. Lo ha sopportato una volta, ma non lo sopporterebbe la seconda. Ecco perché le cose devono rimanere così come sono. E non dimenticare che il mio nome è Don Francisco Urique.' 'Dirò a tutti chi sei realmente,' urlò Thacker, rosso dalla rabbia. Kid prese Thacker per la gola con la sua forte mano sinistra, prese il suo revolver con la mano destra e lo puntò alla bocca del console. Si sentì un rumore di ruote giungere dall'esterno. Kid mise il suo revolver in tasca, e alzando la mano sinistra con il dorso rivolto verso il console tremante, disse: 'C'è un altro motivo perché le cose devono rimanere così come sono. La persona che ho ucciso a Laredo aveva un'aquila sulla mano sinistra.'
pages.english_text: 'The first night I was at Don Urique's house, just after I had gone to bed, my new mother came in and tucked in the blanket. 'My dear boy', she said, and tears dropped from her eyes on my face. I have had very little to do with mothers in my life, but I think that this mother must be kept fooled. She stood it once, but she won't stand it twice. That's why things must be just as they are. And don't forget that my name is Don Francisco Urique.' 'I'll tell everybody today who you really are,' cried Thacker, red with anger. Kid took Thacker by the throat with his strong left hand, drew out his revolver with his right hand and aimed the revolver at the consul's mouth. There came a sound of wheels from outside. Kid put his revolver into his pocket, and holding up his left hand with its back toward the trembling consul said: 'There is one more reason why things must remain as they are. The fellow whom I killed in Laredo had an eagle on his left hand.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Ho avuto poco a che fare con le madri nella mia vita.'
translation.answer: I have had very little to do with mothers in my life.

comprehension.question: Why did Kid not want to reveal the truth to Donna Urique?
comprehension.answer: Because she had already lost one son and would not survive another loss.

comprehension.question: What final reason did Kid give for keeping the secret?
comprehension.answer: The man he killed in Laredo had an eagle tattoo on his left hand – meaning that was the real son, so Kid is now the only one with that mark.

pages.italian_text: In quel momento la carrozza di Don Urique si fermò davanti alla porta del consolato. 'Dove sei, mio caro figlio?' chiamò Donna Urique. 'Sono qui, cara mamma,' rispose Kid, o, come lo ha sempre chiamato Thacker da allora, Don Francisco Urique.
pages.english_text: At that moment the carriage of Don Urique stopped at the door of the consulate. 'Where are you, dear son?' called Donna Urique. 'I am here, dear mother,' answered Kid, or, as Thacker has ever since called him, Don Francisco Urique.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Sono qui, cara mamma.'
translation.answer: I am here, dear mother.

comprehension.question: How did Kid respond to Donna Urique's call?
comprehension.answer: He answered 'I am here, dear mother.'

comprehension.question: What name did Thacker call Kid from then on?
comprehension.answer: Don Francisco Urique.

overall_questions.question: What happened in the gambling house in Laredo?
overall_questions.answer: Kid shot and killed a young man after being grazed by his bullet.

overall_questions.question: Why did Kid leave the train and take a horse?
overall_questions.answer: He didn't trust electricity (telegraph poles) and felt safer on horseback.

overall_questions.question: Who was Thacker?
overall_questions.answer: The U.S. consul at Buenas Tierras, a drunkard with a scheme.

overall_questions.question: What was Thacker's plan for Kid?
overall_questions.answer: To have Kid pose as the long-lost son of Don Urique, then steal $100,000 from the safe and split it.

overall_questions.question: What identifying mark did the lost son have?
overall_questions.answer: A tattoo of a flying eagle on the back of his left hand.

overall_questions.question: Did Kid go along with Thacker's plan?
overall_questions.answer: Initially yes, but after being treated as a son, he changed his mind.

overall_questions.question: Why did Kid refuse to steal the money?
overall_questions.answer: He was moved by Donna Urique's love and did not want to hurt her again.

overall_questions.question: How did Kid threaten Thacker when Thacker wanted to expose him?
overall_questions.answer: He grabbed Thacker by the throat and aimed a revolver at his mouth.

overall_questions.question: What did Kid reveal about the man he killed in Laredo?
overall_questions.answer: That man had an eagle tattoo on his left hand – meaning he was the real son.

overall_questions.question: What name does Kid ultimately go by?
overall_questions.answer: Don Francisco Urique.

pages.italian_text: Jesse Fulton era in piedi sulla porta dell'ufficio di Tom Brackett. Era arrivato lì dopo un viaggio di due settimane da Kansas City, camminando per quasi tutto il tragitto. Aveva i vestiti logori e sporchi, e la stanchezza si vedeva sul suo viso. Durante il viaggio non aveva mangiato molto. Si era tagliato mentre si faceva la barba e ora c'era una profonda cicatrice sulla sua guancia. Era stanco, ma i suoi occhi grigi erano ancora pieni di una certa luce. Si guardò intorno nell'ufficio, poi entrò.
pages.english_text: Jesse Fulton was standing at the door of Tom Brackett's office. He had arrived there after a two-week journey from Kansas City, walking almost all the way. His clothes were worn and dirty, and tiredness showed on his face. He hadn't eaten much during the trip. He had cut himself while shaving and now there was a deep scar on his cheek. He was tired, but his gray eyes still held a certain light. He looked around the office, then entered.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Era arrivato lì dopo un viaggio di due settimane da Kansas City.'
translation.answer: He had arrived there after a two-week journey from Kansas City.

comprehension.question: How had Jesse traveled to Tom Brackett's office?
comprehension.answer: He walked almost all the way from Kansas City for two weeks.

comprehension.question: What mark did Jesse have on his cheek?
comprehension.answer: A deep scar from cutting himself while shaving.

pages.italian_text: Brackett stava finendo una telefonata. Jesse lo osservava. Era un uomo corpulento di quarantacinque anni, con i capelli scuri e un volto determinato. Aveva l'aspetto di un uomo d'affari solido e capace. Brackett posò il ricevitore e si appoggiò sulla sedia, guardando Jesse con i suoi piccoli occhi azzurri, sospettosi e poco amichevoli. 'Sì?' disse all'improvviso. 'Cosa vuoi?' Jesse si avvicinò al bancone che divideva l'ufficio. 'Credo tu non mi abbia riconosciuto, Tom. Sono Jesse Fulton.' 'Cosa?' disse Brackett. 'Sì, sono io, ed Ella ti manda il suo affetto.' Brackett si alzò e si avvicinò, guardando Jesse attentamente, cercando di riconoscere il cognato che ricordava.
pages.english_text: Brackett was finishing a phone call. Jesse watched him. He was a stout man of forty-five, with dark hair and a determined face. He looked like a solid, capable businessman. Brackett put down the receiver and leaned back in his chair, looking at Jesse with his small, clear blue eyes, suspicious and unfriendly. 'Yes?' he said suddenly. 'What do you want?' Jesse moved closer to the counter that divided the office. 'I guess you don't recognize me, Tom. I am Jesse Fulton.' 'What?' said Brackett. 'Yes, I am, and Ella sends you her love.' Brackett rose and came closer, staring at Jesse, trying to recognize the brother-in-law he remembered.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Ella ti manda il suo affetto.'
translation.answer: Ella sends you her love.

comprehension.question: What was Tom Brackett's initial reaction to Jesse?
comprehension.answer: He was suspicious and unfriendly, and didn't recognize him at first.

comprehension.question: How was Brackett described physically?
comprehension.answer: Stout, forty-five years old, dark hair, determined face, small clear blue eyes.

pages.italian_text: Jesse era alto, circa trent'anni, con bei lineamenti e un corpo diritto, ma era troppo magro e stanco. Brackett pensava che sembrava una fotografia sbiadita dell'originale. 'Sì, ti credo, sei tu,' disse finalmente, 'ma sei cambiato tanto.' Jesse si sentì ferito. 'Certo, sono passati cinque anni. E mi hai visto solo qualche volta.' Poi aggiunse tra sé: 'Non sono mica un cadavere.' Brackett continuò a guardarlo con pietà. 'Eri forte e in salute. Avrai perso peso?' Jesse rimase in silenzio, sforzandosi di non esplodere. La pausa divenne imbarazzante. Brackett arrossì. 'Scusami,' disse, alzando il bancone. 'Entra. Siediti. Santo Cielo, ragazzo, sono felice di vederti. Avevi solo un aspetto così esausto...'
pages.english_text: Jesse was tall, about thirty years old, with good features and an erect body, but he was too thin and tired. Brackett thought he looked like a faded photograph of the original. 'Yes, I believe you are you,' he said finally, 'but you've certainly changed.' Jesse felt hurt. 'Sure, it's been five years. And you only saw me a couple of times.' Then he said to himself: 'I'm not a corpse, you know.' Brackett continued to look at him with pity. 'You were strong and healthy. You've lost weight, I guess?' Jesse remained silent, straining not to explode. The pause became awkward. Brackett blushed. 'Excuse me,' he said, lifting the counter. 'Come in. Sit down. Good God, boy, I'm glad to see you. You just looked so exhausted...'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Non sono mica un cadavere.'
translation.answer: I'm not a corpse, you know.

comprehension.question: How did Jesse react to Brackett's remarks about his appearance?
comprehension.answer: He felt hurt but controlled himself, though he was burning with rage inside.

comprehension.question: What did Brackett do after the awkward pause?
comprehension.answer: He apologized, lifted the counter, and invited Jesse to sit down.

pages.italian_text: 'Va tutto bene,' mormorò Jesse, passandosi una mano tra i capelli spettinati. 'Perché zoppichi?' chiese Brackett. 'Ho calpestato una pietra affilata, ha bucato la mia scarpa.' Jesse nascose i piedi sotto la sedia, vergognandosi delle sue scarpe distrutte. Brackett evitò di guardare i suoi piedi. Sapeva cosa tormentava il giovane. 'Beh,' iniziò Brackett, 'dimmi tutto, come sta Ella?' 'Oh, sta bene,' rispose Jesse distrattamente. 'E i bambini?' 'Oh, stanno bene... il più giovane deve indossare un sostegno. Non può correre, purtroppo. Ma è intelligente.' Brackett annuì. Ci fu silenzio. Jesse si sentiva a disagio. Brackett si chinò e mise una mano sul ginocchio di Jesse. 'Ella non mi ha mai detto che le cose erano così difficili per voi. Avrei potuto aiutare.'
pages.english_text: 'It's all right,' Jesse murmured, running his hand through his uncombed hair. 'Why are you limping?' asked Brackett. 'I stepped on a sharp stone; it made a hole in my shoe.' Jesse hid his feet under the chair, ashamed of his ruined shoes. Brackett kept his eyes off Jesse's feet. He knew what was troubling the young man. 'Well,' Brackett began, 'tell me everything, how's Ella?' 'Oh, she's quite all right,' Jesse replied absently. 'And the kids?' 'Oh, they're fine… the young one has to wear a brace. He can't run around. But he's smart.' Brackett nodded. There was silence. Jesse felt awkward. Brackett leaned forward and put his hand on Jesse's knee. 'Ella didn't tell me things were so bad for you. I might have helped.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Il più giovane deve indossare un sostegno.'
translation.answer: The young one has to wear a brace.

comprehension.question: Why was Jesse limping?
comprehension.answer: He had stepped on a sharp stone that made a hole in his shoe.

comprehension.question: What health problem did Jesse's younger son have?
comprehension.answer: He had to wear a brace and couldn't run around.

pages.italian_text: Jesse spiegò che erano molto poveri, che non avevano più il sussidio di disoccupazione. Brackett chiese del lavoro che Jesse aveva perso. Jesse raccontò che sei anni di disoccupazione gli avevano fatto perdere le sue capacità come stampatore. 'L'unico lavoro che ho fatto è stato trasportare pietre. Quando ho trovato un lavoro in primavera, avrei dovuto essere uno specialista, ma non lo ero più. E hanno nuove macchine. Mi hanno fatto fuori in una settimana.' Brackett disse che avrebbe prestato dei soldi e che Jesse doveva tornare a casa e cercare di riprendere il sussidio. 'E poi?' chiese Jesse. 'E poi aspetti. Non sei un vecchio. Non hai il diritto di buttare via la tua vita.' 'No!' saltò su Jesse. 'Non ci credo più. Non troverò un lavoro, proprio come tu non riavrai il tuo negozio di ferramenta.'
pages.english_text: Jesse explained that they were very poor, that they no longer received unemployment relief. Brackett asked about the job Jesse had lost. Jesse told him that six years of unemployment had made him lose his skills as a printer. 'The only work I've had was carrying stones. When I got a job this spring, I was supposed to be a first-class specialist, but I wasn't anymore. And they have new machines now. I was laid off in a week.' Brackett said he would lend some money and Jesse should go home and try to get the relief back. 'And then what?' asked Jesse. 'And then wait. You aren't an old man. You've got no right to throw your life away.' 'No!' Jesse jumped up. 'I don't believe that anymore. I will not get a job, just as you won't get back your hardware store.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Mi hanno fatto fuori in una settimana.'
translation.answer: I was laid off in a week.

comprehension.question: What job had Jesse lost due to lack of skills?
comprehension.answer: A printing job (he was supposed to be a first-class specialist but wasn't anymore).

comprehension.question: What did Brackett suggest Jesse do?
comprehension.answer: Go home, get unemployment relief, and wait for another job.

pages.italian_text: Jesse era disperato. Poi raccontò di un uomo di nome Egbert che lavorava per Brackett, guidando camion di nitroglicerina. Egbert aveva detto che si guadagnava un dollaro al miglio, fino a seicento dollari al mese. 'Voglio quel lavoro, Tom.' Brackett si oppose con forza. 'Non sai cosa sia. Non è dinamite. È nitroglicerina! Basta alzare la voce e salta tutto. Egbert è morto ieri notte. Il ponte è esploso. Non c'è più né camion né cadavere.' Jesse ascoltò, ma non cambiò idea. 'Non mi importa. Devo farlo. Non posso più vivere così. Voglio camminare per strada a testa alta. Voglio comprare un paio di scarpe, un vestito, delle caramelle per i bambini. Voglio che Ella mangi carne tre volte a settimana.'
pages.english_text: Jesse was desperate. Then he told about a man named Egbert who worked for Brackett, driving nitroglycerin trucks. Egbert had said you earn a dollar per mile, up to six hundred dollars a month. 'I want that job, Tom.' Brackett opposed strongly. 'You don't know what it is. It's not dynamite. It's nitroglycerin! Just raise your voice and it blows up. Egbert died last night. The bridge exploded. There's no truck, no corpse.' Jesse listened but didn't change his mind. 'I don't care. I have to do it. I can't live like this anymore. I want to walk down the street with my head up. I want to buy a pair of shoes, a suit, some candy for the kids. I want Ella to eat meat three times a week.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Basta alzare la voce e salta tutto.'
translation.answer: Just raise your voice and it blows up.

comprehension.question: Who was Egbert and what happened to him?
comprehension.answer: He was a nitroglycerin truck driver for Brackett; he died the previous night when the bridge exploded.

comprehension.question: What did Jesse say he wanted to buy with the money?
comprehension.answer: Shoes, a suit, candy for the kids, and for Ella to eat meat three times a week.

pages.italian_text: Brackett cercò di dissuaderlo, dicendogli che sarebbe vissuto nell'ansia, che ogni giorno libero sarebbe stato un tormento in attesa del turno successivo. Jesse rise. 'Sarò felice. Can terò. Mi sentirò orgoglioso di me stesso per la prima volta in sei anni.' Brackett alla fine cedette. 'Va bene, ti prenderò. Dio mi aiuti. Se sei pronto a guidare stanotte, puoi guidare stanotte.' Jesse non riuscì a parlare. Le lacrime gli scorrevano sul viso. Brackett disse che avrebbe mandato un telegramma a Ella dicendole che Jesse aveva un lavoro, e che le avrebbe mandato dei soldi. 'Avrai qualche dollaro – cioè, se duri una settimana, stupido.' Jesse annuì, con il cuore che batteva forte. Uscì dall'ufficio, zoppicando, ma con il cuore pieno di gioia. Sussurrò a se stesso: 'Sono l'uomo più felice del mondo.'
pages.english_text: Brackett tried to dissuade him, saying he would live in anxiety, that every free day would be torment waiting for the next shift. Jesse laughed. 'I'll be happy. I'll sing. I'll feel proud of myself for the first time in six years.' Brackett finally gave in. 'All right, I'll take you on. God help me. If you're ready to drive tonight, you can drive tonight.' Jesse couldn't speak. Tears ran down his face. Brackett said he would send a telegram to Ella telling her Jesse had a job, and that he would send her money. 'You'll have a few dollars – that is, if you last a week, you fool.' Jesse nodded, his heart beating wildly. He left the office, limping, but with his heart full of joy. He whispered to himself: 'I am the happiest man on Earth.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Mi sentirò orgoglioso di me stesso per la prima volta in sei anni.'
translation.answer: I'll feel proud of myself for the first time in six years.

comprehension.question: What did Jesse say about how he would feel if he got the job?
comprehension.answer: He would be happy, sing, and feel proud for the first time in six years.

comprehension.question: What did Brackett finally agree to do?
comprehension.answer: He agreed to give Jesse the job driving the nitroglycerin truck that very night.

pages.italian_text: Brackett rimase seduto a guardare fino a quando Jesse svoltò l'angolo del vicolo e scomparve. Poi si appoggiò all'indietro sulla sedia. Il suo cuore batteva dolorosamente. Ascoltava ogni battito. Sedeva fermo, con la testa tra le mani.
pages.english_text: Brackett sat watching till finally Jesse turned the corner of the alley and disappeared. Then he leaned back in his chair. His heart was beating painfully. He listened to it as it beat. He sat still, gripping his head in his hands.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Sedeva fermo, con la testa tra le mani.'
translation.answer: He sat still, gripping his head in his hands.

comprehension.question: How did Brackett feel after Jesse left?
comprehension.answer: His heart was beating painfully; he sat still with his head in his hands, likely feeling guilt or despair.

comprehension.question: What does the ending suggest about Brackett's emotions?
comprehension.answer: He is deeply troubled and possibly regrets agreeing to send Jesse to such a dangerous job.

overall_questions.question: How did Jesse get to Tom Brackett's office?
overall_questions.answer: He walked from Kansas City for two weeks.

overall_questions.question: What was Jesse's profession before becoming unemployed?
overall_questions.answer: Printer.

overall_questions.question: Why did Jesse lose his last printing job?
overall_questions.answer: He had lost his skills after six years of unemployment, and the company had new machines.

overall_questions.question: What job did Jesse want from Brackett?
overall_questions.answer: Driving a truck that transports nitroglycerin.

overall_questions.question: How much did the job pay per mile?
overall_questions.answer: One dollar per mile, up to five or six hundred dollars a month.

overall_questions.question: What happened to Egbert, the previous driver?
overall_questions.answer: He died the night before when his truck exploded on a bridge.

overall_questions.question: Why did Jesse insist on taking such a dangerous job?
overall_questions.answer: He was desperate, his family was starving, and he wanted to regain his dignity and provide for them.

overall_questions.question: What did Jesse say he wanted to buy with the money?
overall_questions.answer: Shoes, a suit, candy for the kids, and for Ella to eat meat three times a week.

overall_questions.question: What did Jesse whisper to himself as he left?
overall_questions.answer: 'I am the happiest man on Earth.'

overall_questions.question: How did Brackett feel at the end of the story?
overall_questions.answer: He was deeply troubled, his heart beating painfully, sitting with his head in his hands.

pages.italian_text: Ero un giovane uomo. Ero appena ritornato dalla Cina, ma siccome la mia famiglia era via, sono andato in campagna a stare da uno zio. Quando arrivai sul luogo, lo trovai chiuso perché mio zio si trovava nel Sud della Francia; siccome sarebbe dovuto ritornare tra alcuni giorni, decisi di soggiornare al Royal George, una locanda molto dignitosa, e attendere il suo ritorno. Il primo giorno lo trascorsi abbastanza bene; ma nel pomeriggio la monotonia del luogo, in cui ero l'unico visitatore, iniziò ad annoiarmi, e il mattino seguente dopo una tarda colazione, uscii con l'intenzione di fare una camminata a passo spedito.
pages.english_text: I was a young man. I had just come back from China, but since my family was away, I went to the country to stay with an uncle.
