When I got down to the place I found it closed because my uncle was in the South of France; because he was supposed to come back in a few days I decided to stay at the Royal George, a very decent inn, and await his return. The first day I passed well enough; but in the evening the dullness of the place, in which I was the only visitor, began to bore me, and the next morning after a late breakfast I went out with the intention of having a brisk walk.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Decisi di soggiornare al Royal George.'
translation.answer: I decided to stay at the Royal George.

comprehension.question: Why did the narrator go to the country?
comprehension.answer: To stay with his uncle, but the uncle was away in the South of France.

comprehension.question: How did the narrator spend his first day?
comprehension.answer: Well enough, but the dullness of the place began to bore him.

pages.italian_text: Iniziai di ottimo umore, perché la giornata era luminosa e gelida. I villaggi che attraversai erano vecchi e affascinanti. Pranzai lussuosamente con pane e formaggio e una birra nel bar di una piccola locanda, poi decisi di camminare per ancora un po' prima di ritornare indietro. Quando finalmente mi sembrava di essere andato abbastanza lontano, svoltai in un piccolo vicolo, e decisi che avrei trovato la via del ritorno seguendo un altro percorso, facendo affidamento sulla mia piccola bussola. Raggiunsi le paludi, quando una fitta nebbia iniziò gradualmente ad espandersi. Continuai il mio percorso fino alle quattro, mentre si stava facendo sempre più buio. Dovevo ammettere che mi ero perso.
pages.english_text: I started off in excellent spirits, because the day was bright and frosty. The villages through which I passed were old and charming. I lunched luxuriously on bread and cheese and beer in the bar of a small inn, and decided to walk a little further before turning back. When at last I had gone far enough, I turned up a little lane, and decided that I would find my way back by another route, relying on my small compass. I had reached the marshes, when a dense fog began gradually to spread. I continued my course until four o'clock, while it was getting darker and darker. I had to admit that I was lost.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Una fitta nebbia iniziò gradualmente ad espandersi.'
translation.answer: A dense fog began gradually to spread.

comprehension.question: What did the narrator eat for lunch?
comprehension.answer: Bread, cheese, and beer.

comprehension.question: What happened to the narrator at four o'clock?
comprehension.answer: He got lost in the fog and darkness.

pages.italian_text: La bussola non mi era d'aiuto; girovagai miserabilmente, urlando occasionalmente nella speranza di essere sentito da qualche pastore o contadino. Finalmente, per fortuna, i miei piedi si trovarono su una piccola strada che attraversava le paludi, e camminando lentamente e aiutandomi con il bastone riuscii a rimanere su di essa. L'avevo percorsa per una certa distanza quando sentii dei passi che si avvicinavano. Quando ci incontrammo ci fermammo, e lo sconosciuto, un contadino dal villaggio vicino, sentendo la mia situazione, mi accompagnò per quasi un chilometro. Mi portò sulla strada principale e mi diede istruzioni su come raggiungere un villaggio che si trovava a tre chilometri di distanza.
pages.english_text: The compass was no help to me now; I walked about miserably, occasionally shouting in hope of being heard by some passing shepherd or farmer. At last by good luck I found my feet on a small road that was going through the marshes, and by walking slowly and tapping with my stick, I managed to stay on it. I had followed it for some distance when I heard footsteps approaching me. We stopped as we met, and the stranger, a peasant from the neighboring village, hearing of my situation, walked back with me for nearly a mile. He put me on the main road and gave me instructions how to reach a village that was located three miles away.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Riuscii a rimanere su di essa.'
translation.answer: I managed to stay on it.

comprehension.question: Who helped the narrator find his way?
comprehension.answer: A peasant from the neighboring village.

comprehension.question: How far was the nearest village?
comprehension.answer: Three miles away.

pages.italian_text: Ero così stanco che tre chilometri mi sembravano dieci, e oltre a questo, non lontano dalla strada vidi una finestra illuminata da una fioca luce. Indicai la finestra, ma il mio accompagnatore scosse la testa e si guardò intorno con disagio. 'Non troverai niente di buono là,' disse lui. 'Perché no?' gli chiesi. 'C'è qualcosa in quella casa, signore,' rispose. 'Cosa sia non lo so. Alcuni dicono sia una cosa pazza, altri dicono si tratti di una specie di animale; ma qualunque cosa sia, non è bella da vedere.' 'Beh. Allora ci vado,' dissi io, 'buonasera.' Lui se ne andò fischiettando finché il rumore dei suoi passi svanì in lontananza, e io seguii la strada che mi aveva indicato. Ma avevo freddo ed ero stanco e decisi di tornare verso la casa.
pages.english_text: I was so tired that three miles sounded like ten, and besides, not far from the road I saw a dimly lighted window. I pointed to the window, but my companion shook his head and looked round him uneasily. 'You won't get any good there,' he said. 'Why not?' I asked. 'There's something in there, sir,' he answered. 'What it is I don't know. Some say that it's a poor mad thing, others say it's a kind of animal; but whatever it is, it isn't good to see.' 'Well, I'll go on then,' I said, 'good night.' He went back whistling until the sound of his footsteps faded in the distance, and I followed the road he had indicated. But I was cold and tired and decided to go back toward the house.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Non troverai niente di buono là.'
translation.answer: You won't get any good there.

comprehension.question: What did the peasant warn about the house with the dimly lit window?
comprehension.answer: That something strange lives there, either a mad thing or an animal, and it's not good to see.

comprehension.question: What did the narrator decide to do after the peasant left?
comprehension.answer: He was cold and tired, so he decided to go back toward the house.

pages.italian_text: Dal suo interno non traspariva alcuna luce o suono. Bussai leggermente alla porta. Improvvisamente si aprì e una donna alta e vecchia, con una candela in mano, mi salutò. 'Cosa vuoi?' chiese lei. 'Mi sono perso; voglio arrivare ad Ashville.' 'Non la conosco,' disse la vecchia signora. Stava per chiudere la porta quando un uomo apparve da una stanza che si trovava a fianco dell'atrio e si avvicinò. 'Ashville si trova a quindici chilometri da qui,' disse lui con calma. 'Se mi indica il villaggio più vicino, le sarò grato,' dissi io. Lui non rispose, ma scambiò una rapida occhiata con la donna. 'Il villaggio più vicino si trova a tre chilometri,' disse l'uomo voltandosi verso di me e provando ad addolcire la sua voce naturalmente aspra. 'Se vorrà offrirci la sua gradita compagnia, vi offriremo la nostra ospitalità.'
pages.english_text: There was no light and no sound from within. I knocked lightly upon the door. It opened suddenly and a tall old woman, holding a candle, greeted me. 'What do you want?' she asked. 'I've lost my way; I want to get to Ashville.' 'I don't know it,' said the old woman. She wanted to close the door when a man appeared from a room at the side of the hall and came toward us. 'Ashville is fifteen miles from here,' he said calmly. 'If you will direct me to the nearest village, I shall be grateful,' I said. He didn't answer, but exchanged a quick glance with the woman. 'The nearest village is three miles away,' he said turning to me and trying to soften a naturally harsh voice. 'If you will give us the pleasure of your company, we'll offer you our hospitality.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Scambiò una rapida occhiata con la donna.'
translation.answer: He exchanged a quick glance with the woman.

comprehension.question: What did the old woman say when the narrator asked for Ashville?
comprehension.answer: She didn't know it.

comprehension.question: What did the man offer the narrator?
comprehension.answer: Hospitality and dinner, inviting him to stay.

pages.italian_text: Io esitai. Erano certamente una coppia molto strana, e l'atrio tetro con l'ombra proiettata dalle candele era poco più invitante dell'oscurità all'esterno. 'Lei è molto gentile,' mormorai, 'ma -' 'Entri.' Disse lui velocemente. 'Chiudi la porta, Anna.' Senza accorgermene, mi trovai in casa e la vecchia signora, mormorando tra sé, aveva chiuso la porta dietro di me. Con la strana sensazione di essere intrappolato, seguii il padrone di casa nella stanza, presi una sedia e riscaldai le mie dita gelate davanti al fuoco. 'La cena è quasi pronta,' disse il vecchio, 'se volete scusarmi.' Annuii e lui uscì dalla stanza. Un minuto più tardi sentii delle voci: la sua e quella della vecchia signora e, ne ero sicuro, una terza.
pages.english_text: I hesitated. They were certainly a strange looking couple, and the gloomy hall with the shadows thrown by the candle looked hardly more inviting than the darkness outside. 'You are very kind,' I murmured, 'but - ' 'Come in,' he said quickly. 'Shut the door, Anna.' Almost before I knew it I was standing inside and the old woman, muttering to herself, had closed the door behind me. With a strange feeling of being trapped I followed my host into the room, took a chair and warmed my frozen fingers at the fire. 'Dinner is almost ready,' said the old man, 'if you will excuse me.' I nodded and he left the room. A minute later I heard voices: his and the old woman's and, I was sure, a third.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Senza accorgermene, mi trovai in casa.'
translation.answer: Almost before I knew it, I was standing inside the house.

comprehension.question: How did the narrator feel when he entered the house?
comprehension.answer: He had a strange feeling of being trapped.

comprehension.question: What did the narrator hear after the old man left the room?
comprehension.answer: Voices: the old man's, the old woman's, and a third voice.

pages.italian_text: Prima di finire di ispezionare la stanza lui ritornò, e mi guardò con lo stesso strano sguardo che avevo notato prima. 'Noi saremo in tre a cena,' disse. 'Noi due e mio figlio.' Annuii di nuovo. 'Suppongo che non le dispiaccia cenare al buio,' disse. 'Niente affatto,' risposi, nascondendo il mio stupore al meglio che potevo, 'ma veramente non vorrei disturbare. Se mi permettete - ' 'È raro che noi abbiamo compagnia,' disse, 'e ora che Lei è qui, vogliamo che Lei rimanga. Mio figlio ha dei problemi agli occhi, e non sopporta la luce. Ah, ecco qui Anna.' Mentre lui parlava, la vecchia signora entrò, mi guardò e iniziò a stendere la tovaglia, mentre il padrone di casa prendendo una sedia si sedette guardando in silenzio il fuoco.
pages.english_text: Before I had finished my inspection of the room he returned, and looked at me with the same strange look I noticed before. 'There will be three of us at dinner,' he said. 'That is two of us and my son.' I nodded again. 'I suppose you don't mind dining in the dark,' he said. 'Not at all,' I answered, hiding my surprise as well as I could, 'but really I don't want to intrude. If you will allow me - ' 'It's seldom that we have company,' he said, 'and now that you're here we want you to stay. My son has some eye problems, and he can't stand the light. Ah, here's Anna.' As he spoke the old woman entered, glanced at me and began to lay the tablecloth, while my host, taking a chair, sat looking silently into the fire.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Mio figlio ha dei problemi agli occhi, e non sopporta la luce.'
translation.answer: My son has some eye problems, and he can't stand the light.

comprehension.question: Why would they be dining in the dark?
comprehension.answer: Because the son has eye problems and cannot stand the light.

comprehension.question: Who else would be at dinner?
comprehension.answer: The narrator, the old man, and his son.

pages.italian_text: Dopo aver apparecchiato il tavolo, la vecchia signora arrivò con un paio di polli già tagliati nel piatto, e preparando tre sedie, lasciò la stanza. Il vecchio esitò un attimo, e poi, alzandosi dalla sedia, sistemò un largo schermo davanti al fuoco e lentamente spense le candele. 'Le vacanze di un uomo cieco,' disse, e procedendo a tentoni verso la porta, la aprì. Qualcuno entrò nella stanza con lui, e in modo lento e incerto si sedette a tavola. E la voce più strana che avessi mai sentito ruppe il silenzio. 'Una notte fredda,' disse piano. 'Sì, veramente,' risposi e, con luce o senza luce, iniziai a mangiare con appetito. Era piuttosto difficile mangiare al buio, ed era evidente dal comportamento dei miei compagni invisibili che anche loro non erano abituati a cenare in queste circostanze.
pages.english_text: When the table was set, the old woman brought in a pair of chickens ready carved in the dish, and placing three chairs, left the room. The old man hesitated a moment, and then, rising from his chair, placed a large screen in front of the fire and slowly extinguished the candles. 'Blind man's holiday,' he said, and groping his way to the door opened it. Somebody came back into the room with him, and in a slow, uncertain manner took a seat at the table. And the strangest voice I have ever heard broke the silence. 'A cold night,' it said slowly. 'It sure is,' I answered and, light or no light, started eating with appetite. It was somewhat difficult eating in the dark, and it was evident from the behavior of my invisible companions that they also were not used to dining under such circumstances.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Le vacanze di un uomo cieco.'
translation.answer: Blind man's holiday.

comprehension.question: What did the old man do with the candles?
comprehension.answer: He slowly extinguished them after placing a screen in front of the fire.

comprehension.question: What did the strange voice say?
comprehension.answer: 'A cold night.'

pages.italian_text: Cenammo in silenzio finché la vecchia signora entrò nella stanza con dei dolci e li mise sul tavolo. 'Lei è uno straniero da queste parti?' chiese ancora una volta la strana voce. 'Sì,' risposi e mormorai qualcosa riguardo alla mia fortuna di essermi imbattuto casualmente in una cena così deliziosa. 'Imbattersi casualmente è la parola giusta,' disse la voce con fermezza. 'Hai dimenticato il vino, padre.' 'È vero,' disse il vecchio, alzandosi. 'È una bottiglia per il 'Celebre' di oggi! La prendo io.' Si avviò a tentoni verso la porta, e chiudendola dietro di sé, mi lasciò solo con il mio vicino invisibile. C'era qualcosa di molto strano nell'intera faccenda che, devo confessare, mi faceva sentire molto a disagio.
pages.english_text: We ate in silence until the old woman came into the room with some sweets and put them upon the table. 'Are you a stranger around here?' asked the strange voice again. 'Yes,' I answered and murmured something about my luck in stumbling upon such a good dinner. 'Stumbling is a very good word for it,' said the voice firmly. 'You have forgotten the wine, father.' 'So I have,' said the old man, rising. 'It's a bottle of the 'Celebrated' today! I'll get it.' He groped his way to the door, and closing it behind him, left me alone with my invisible neighbor. There was something so strange about the whole business that I must confess I felt very uneasy.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Mi faceva sentire molto a disagio.'
translation.answer: It made me feel very uneasy.

comprehension.question: What did the strange voice say about 'stumbling'?
comprehension.answer: That it was a very good word for it.

comprehension.question: How did the narrator feel when left alone with the invisible neighbor?
comprehension.answer: Very uneasy.

pages.italian_text: Sentii l'uomo accanto a me posare la sua forchetta e il suo cucchiaio, e mi era quasi sembrato di vedere un paio di occhi selvaggi brillare nell'oscurità, come quelli di un gatto. Con un crescente senso di disagio, spinsi all'indietro la sedia, lo schermo cadde sbattendo, e nella luce del fuoco vidi il viso della creatura davanti a me. Senza respiro mi alzai dalla sedia e rimasi in piedi accanto ad essa con i pugni stretti. Nel rosso bagliore del fuoco sembrava così diabolico. Per alcuni istanti, ci guardammo a vicenda in silenzio; poi si aprì la porta e il vecchio ritornò. Rimase scioccato quando vide la calda luce del fuoco, e poi, avvicinandosi al tavolo, appoggiò meccanicamente un paio di bottiglie.
pages.english_text: I heard the man opposite lay down his fork and spoon, and it almost seemed to me that I saw a pair of wild eyes shining through the darkness like a cat's. With a growing sense of uneasiness I pushed my chair back, the screen fell over with a bang, and in the light of the fire I saw the face of the creature in front of me. Breathless I got up from the chair and stood with clenched fists beside it. In the red glow of the fire it looked so devilish. For a few moments we looked at each other in silence; then the door opened and the old man returned. He stood shocked as he saw the warm firelight, and then, approaching the table, mechanically put down a couple of bottles.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Lo schermo cadde sbattendo.'
translation.answer: The screen fell over with a bang.

comprehension.question: What did the narrator see when the screen fell?
comprehension.answer: The face of the creature (the son) in the firelight, which looked devilish.

comprehension.question: How did the old man react when he saw the firelight?
comprehension.answer: He stood shocked.

pages.italian_text: 'Vi chiedo scusa,' dissi rassicurato dalla sua presenza, 'ma ho accidentalmente rovesciato lo schermo. Mi permetta di sostituirlo.' 'No,' disse il vecchio gentilmente, 'lasciamo stare. Ne abbiamo abbastanza del buio.' Prese un fiammifero e lentamente accese le candele. Poi vidi che l'uomo davanti a me aveva solo i resti di un volto, un volto orribile dove un occhio, l'unica caratteristica rimanente, ancora brillava. Ne rimasi molto commosso, intuendo una parte della verità. 'Mio figlio è rimasto ferito alcuni anni fa in una casa in fiamme,' disse il vecchio. 'Da allora abbiamo vissuto una vita molto riservata. Quando lei è arrivato alla nostra porta - ossia - mio figlio...' 'Pensai,' disse semplicemente il figlio, 'che sarebbe stato meglio per me non venire a tavola.'
pages.english_text: 'I beg your pardon,' said I reassured by his presence, 'but I have accidentally overturned the screen. Allow me to replace it.' 'No,' said the old man gently, 'let it be. We have had enough of the dark.' He struck a match and slowly lit the candles. Then I saw that the man opposite had the remnant of a face, a horrible face in which one eye, the sole remaining feature, still glittered. I was greatly moved, guessing a part of the truth. 'My son was injured some years ago in a burning house,' said the old man. 'Since then we have lived a very private life. When you came to the door - that is - my son...' 'I thought,' said the son simply, 'that it would be better for me not to come to the dinner table.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Ne abbiamo abbastanza del buio.'
translation.answer: We have had enough of the dark.

comprehension.question: What had happened to the son's face?
comprehension.answer: He had been injured in a burning house years ago, leaving only remnants of a face with one eye remaining.

comprehension.question: Why did the son not want to come to the table?
comprehension.answer: He thought it would be better not to, because of his disfigurement.

pages.italian_text: 'Ma era anche il mio compleanno, e mio padre non voleva saperne che io cenassi da solo, quindi inventammo questo folle piano di cenare al buio. Mi dispiace di averla spaventata.' 'Mi dispiace,' dissi, e allungai attraverso il tavolo per stringergli la mano 'sono uno sciocco, ma è solo al buio che mi hai spaventato.' 'Non vediamo mai nessun amico,' disse il vecchio, 'e la tentazione di avere compagnia era troppa per noi. Inoltre, non so cos'altro avrebbe potuto fare.' 'Niente altro di altrettanto bello, ne sono certo,' dissi. 'Venga,' disse il padrone di casa, quasi allegramente, 'Ora ci conosciamo, avvicini la sua sedia al fuoco e festeggiamo questo compleanno come si deve.'
pages.english_text: 'But it happened to be my birthday, and my father would not hear of my dining alone, so we came up with this foolish plan of dining in the dark. I'm sorry that I have startled you.' 'I am sorry,' said I, and reached across the table to shake his hand 'I am such a fool, but it was only in the dark that you startled me.' 'We never see a friend,' said the old man, 'and the temptation to have company was too much for us. Besides, I don't know what else you could have done.' 'Nothing else just as good, I'm sure,' said I. 'Come,' said my host, almost gaily, 'Now we know each other, draw your chair to the fire and let's celebrate this birthday in a proper fashion.'

translation.question: Translate into English: 'È solo al buio che mi hai spaventato.'
translation.answer: It was only in the dark that you startled me.

comprehension.question: Why did the son agree to the dark dinner?
comprehension.answer: It was his birthday and his father didn't want him to dine alone.

comprehension.question: What did the narrator do after understanding the situation?
comprehension.answer: He shook the son's hand and joined them in celebrating the birthday.

pages.italian_text: Avvicinò un piccolo tavolo al fuoco per i bicchieri e portò una scatola di sigari, e sistemando una sedia per la vecchia serva, le chiese di sedersi e di bere, e presto eravamo tutti così allegri come non avevo mai visto ad una festa. La notte passò così rapidamente che non credemmo alle nostre orecchie quando in un momento di silenzio durante la conversazione, l'orologio nell'atrio suonò la mezzanotte. 'Un ultimo brindisi prima di andare a letto,' disse il padrone di casa, gettando la parte finale del suo sigaro nel fuoco e voltandosi verso il piccolo tavolo. Avevamo già bevuto parecchio prima di questo brindisi, ma c'era qualcosa di impressionante nelle maniere del vecchio mentre si alzava e prendeva il suo bicchiere. La sua alta figura sembrava diventare ancora più alta, e la sua voce risuonò mentre guardava con orgoglio il suo figlio sfigurato. 'Alla salute dei bambini che mio figlio ha salvato!' disse, e svuotò il bicchiere in un unico sorso.
pages.english_text: He drew a small table to the fire for the glasses and brought a box of cigars, and placing a chair for the old servant, asked her to sit down and drink, and we were soon as merry a party as I have ever seen. The night went on so rapidly that we could not believe our ears when in a lull in the conversation a clock in the hall struck twelve. 'A last toast before we go to bed,' said my host, throwing the end of his cigar into the fire and turning to the small table. We had drunk several before this toast, but there was something impressive in the old man's manner as he rose and took up his glass. His tall figure seemed to get taller, and his voice rang as he looked proudly at his disfigured son. 'To the health of the children my boy saved!' he said, and drained his glass in one gulp.

translation.question: Translate into English: 'Svuotò il bicchiere in un unico sorso.'
translation.answer: He drained his glass in one gulp.

comprehension.question: What was the old man's toast?
comprehension.answer: 'To the health of the children my boy saved!'

comprehension.question: What does this toast reveal about the son's injury?
comprehension.answer: He was disfigured while saving children from a burning house.

overall_questions.question: Why did the narrator go to the country?
overall_questions.answer: To stay with his uncle, but the uncle was away in the South of France.

overall_questions.question: Where did the narrator stay while waiting for his uncle?
overall_questions.answer: At the Royal George inn.

overall_questions.question: What happened during his walk?
overall_questions.answer: A dense fog came, he got lost, and a peasant helped him onto the main road.

overall_questions.question: What did the peasant say about the house with the dim light?
overall_questions.answer: That there was something strange inside – either a mad thing or an animal – not good to see.

overall_questions.question: Who answered the door when the narrator knocked?
overall_questions.answer: A tall old woman with a candle, then an old man appeared.

overall_questions.question: Why did the family want to dine in the dark?
overall_questions.answer: Because the son had eye problems and couldn't stand the light (the real reason was his disfigurement).

overall_questions.question: What did the narrator see when the screen fell and the firelight revealed the son's face?
overall_questions.answer: The remnant of a face with one eye remaining, looking horrible and devilish.

overall_questions.question: How did the son become disfigured?
overall_questions.answer: He was injured in a burning house.

overall_questions.question: What did the old man toast to at the end?
overall_questions.answer: The health of the children his son had saved.

overall_questions.question: What is the main theme or message of this story?
overall_questions.answer: Do not judge by appearances; the strange and frightening-looking son is actually a hero who saved children.
